web/dist (build output served by FastAPI, produced by npm run build)

gaps_and_bugs.title: useWebSocket hook + /ws/chat backend route are orphaned (dead code)
gaps_and_bugs.detail: web/frontend/src/hooks/useWebSocket.ts targets /ws/chat and the FastAPI route exists (web/server.py:2931), but no component imports useWebSocket — Grep finds the symbol only in its own file. ChatPage.tsx instead uses REST /api/chat/sessions/{id}/messages. Result: the streaming chat path is dead, the chat 'typing' indicator and live token streaming are unused, and there are two divergent chat backends to maintain. Either delete the hook+route or migrate ChatPage to it.

gaps_and_bugs.title: No co-located tests for App.tsx, the two hooks, or lib (api/utils)
gaps_and_bugs.detail: There are 12 page tests but zero tests for App.tsx (routing, global search, notification toasts), usePlatformUpdates, useWebSocket, lib/api.ts (streamSSE SSE parsing), or lib/utils.ts. The live-update wiring, SSE reader, and search dropdown are the most logic-heavy, least-covered units. A WS-reconnect or SSE-parse regression would ship silently.

gaps_and_bugs.title: HelpPage and DashboardPage docs/UI drift from the claude-CLI rebrand (no API keys)
gaps_and_bugs.detail: Per CLAUDE.md there are NO hosted-LLM API keys (claude CLI only). Yet HelpPage.tsx still documents 'API キー' entry and a provider table (Anthropic/OpenAI/Groq/GitHub Models/Gemini, lines ~137,371-377,409-419), and DashboardPage system-info shows llm_provider plus a hint '設定画面で API キーを保存すると…' (line 737). SettingsPage no longer has any API-key field, so this guidance points users to a non-existent control.

gaps_and_bugs.title: BoardPage 'review' column maps to status 'failed', so failed tasks lose their cancel action and column semantics are misleading
gaps_and_bugs.detail: columns[] maps the レビュー (review) column to statuses:['failed'] (BoardPage.tsx:33). A failed task is neither pending nor running, so handleCancel's guard (task.status==='pending'||'running') hides the cancel button there, and there is no retry action — failed tasks become inert cards labeled 'review' with no way to act on them.

gaps_and_bugs.title: ProposalsPage selectedOrg never follows ?org= query changes after first load
gaps_and_bugs.detail: The effect setSelectedOrg((current)=>current||nextOrg) (line 131) only sets the org when none is selected. If a user is on /proposals and navigates (e.g. from a Dashboard link) to /proposals?org=Other, selectedOrg keeps its old value and the new org param is ignored until reload.

gaps_and_bugs.title: DataPage encodeFilePath splits on '/' only — Windows backslash paths break knowledge file routes
gaps_and_bugs.detail: encodeFilePath splits path on '/' (DataPage.tsx:59-61). If the backend ever returns a Windows-style path with backslashes, the segment encoding produces a wrong URL and GET/PUT/DELETE /api/knowledge/files/{path} fails. Given the repo's known Windows path-separator issues this is a latent cross-platform bug.

gaps_and_bugs.title: DashboardPage velocity chart only counts proposal_created/approved, never rejected; 'created' may stay 0
gaps_and_bugs.detail: velocityData counts operation==='proposal_created' and 'proposal_approved' from execution-history (lines 277-279), but totalProposals/approvalRate elsewhere rely on proposal_approved/proposal_rejected. If the backend emits a different operation name for creation, the 'created' bars silently render as 0 with no error, making the chart misleading.

improvement_ideas.title: Decide ChatPage transport: either delete useWebSocket+/ws/chat or migrate ChatPage to streaming
improvement_ideas.rationale: Eliminates a dead, divergent second chat backend. If streaming UX is desired, wiring ChatPage to useWebSocket gives token-by-token responses + the existing typing indicator; otherwise removing both removes maintenance burden and confusion.

improvement_ideas.title: Add tests for App.tsx, usePlatformUpdates, useWebSocket, and lib/api streamSSE
improvement_ideas.rationale: These are the highest-logic, zero-coverage units (routing, WS reconnect, SSE line buffering). The /add-web-page convention already enforces co-located tests for pages; extend the same discipline to hooks/lib to protect the live-update and streaming paths.

improvement_ideas.title: Sweep claude-CLI rebrand doc drift out of HelpPage and DashboardPage
improvement_ideas.rationale: Remove the API-key/provider guidance that no longer matches the keyless claude-CLI runtime (HelpPage provider table + DashboardPage 'save API key' hint). Aligns the in-product onboarding with reality and prevents users hunting for a removed setting.

improvement_ideas.title: Centralize REST type definitions instead of re-declaring per page
improvement_ideas.rationale: Organization, Proposal, GoalHistoryItem, RuntimeStatus, SettingsData etc. are redeclared (and subtly diverge) across OrgsPage/AnalyzePage/ProposalsPage/GoalsPage/DataPage/SettingsPage/DashboardPage. A shared web/frontend/src/lib/types.ts (or generated from FastAPI OpenAPI) removes drift and the manual normalizeGoalHistoryItem shims.

improvement_ideas.title: Replace 10s Dashboard polling and per-page reloads with the existing /ws/updates feed
improvement_ideas.rationale: usePlatformUpdates already streams entity/operation events globally but is only used for notifications. Feeding it into DashboardPage/BoardPage/SessionsPage state would replace setInterval polling (Dashboard) and manual 更新 buttons with push updates, cutting redundant requests and giving truly live boards.

improvement_ideas.title: Add an end-to-end Playwright smoke flow (init -> create org -> analyze -> approve)
improvement_ideas.rationale: The MCP Playwright server is already configured; a scripted happy-path across the analyze->propose->approve flow would catch route/contract regressions (e.g. the ?org= selectedOrg bug) that unit tests with mocked api() cannot.

improvement_ideas.title: Generalize BoardPage review column with retry + per-status actions
improvement_ideas.rationale: Map the review column to a real reviewable state and add retry/requeue and cancel actions for failed tasks so the human work board can actually act on failures rather than displaying inert cards.

flows_participated.role_in_flow: `pantheon init` bootstraps ~/.pantheon; epilog + per-command 'next step' hints chain init -> org add -> analyze -> proposals -> approve.
`pantheon org add` (cmd_org_add) creates an Organization from default or template and binds a target repo; org list/show/remove manage it.

cmd_analyze generates+persists proposals; cmd_proposals/cmd_proposal_show list/inspect; cmd_approve/cmd_proposal_apply (claude-gated) apply via OrchestratorAgent -> PR/branch; cmd_proposal_reject closes them; cmd_query inspects the SQLite store.
cmd_goal_run feeds a natural-language goal into AbstractGoalPipeline; cmd_goal_status surfaces the GoalLibrary history.
commands/session.py is the entire user-facing surface for SessionOrchestrator (wmux/cmux/headless), including rate-limit watch/resume and connectivity doctor.
cmd_chat is the recommended entry; launches agents.chat_agent.run_chat which internally reaches the other flows via slash commands.
cmd_platform_run_all runs SelfImprovementLoop priority-ordered across orgs; cmd_daemon_start/stop/status manage the autonomous background runner (core._daemon_runner).
orchestration analyze/history/capabilities/self-review + agent status expose Pre-Task routing, pattern stats, capability gaps, and skill proficiency.
cmd_serve launches the FastAPI backend (web.server.run_server) that serves the React frontend.
platform backup/restore snapshot ~/.pantheon via BackupManager; doctor diagnoses/auto-fixes platform health.

state_touched: ~/.pantheon/gui_settings.json (SETTINGS_FILE; legacy provider/llm_provider settings read by main._load_gui_settings)

gaps_and_bugs.title: `proposal apply` (non-approve path) does not gate on the claude CLI before mutating state
gaps_and_bugs.detail: cmd_proposal_apply IS wired with require_api_key and calls it first (main.cmd_proposal_apply passes _require_api_key), so apply is gated. However the gate prints/exit only checks claude_available() globally; there is no per-org/per-repo validation that org.target_repo_path still exists before apply. If target_repo_path was deleted, apply falls back to Path('.') (current working dir) silently — the executor could modify the wrong repo. Same fallback `Path('.')` exists in cmd_analyze (commands/org.py:175) and cmd_proposal_apply (commands/org.py:323).

gaps_and_bugs.title: Orchestration commands reach into a private attribute `store._records`
gaps_and_bugs.detail: cmd_orchestration_history and cmd_orchestration_self_review iterate `list(store._records)` directly (commands/orchestration.py:35,106) instead of a public accessor on OrchestrationPatternStore. This couples the CLI to store internals and will silently break (empty history) if the field is renamed.

gaps_and_bugs.title: Dead/vestigial hosted-LLM provider config still present in main.py
gaps_and_bugs.detail: main.py keeps SETTINGS_FILE, _PROVIDER_KEY_MAPPING, _load_gui_settings, _resolve_llm_provider for anthropic/openai/groq/etc. The project has NO hosted-LLM path (all generation via local claude CLI per CLAUDE.md). _resolve_llm_provider is defined but never called anywhere in the CLI dispatch — pure dead code that misleads readers into thinking API keys matter.

gaps_and_bugs.title: `platform config` parent has no handler when invoked without `set`
gaps_and_bugs.detail: register() sets handler_name=cmd_platform_config on the parent and adds a `set` subparser WITHOUT required=True (commands/platform.py:363). Running `pantheon platform config` works (lists config), but the optional subparser means `config` and `config set` share a parent whose dest isn't required — inconsistent with every other group that uses required=True, and easy to mis-evolve.

gaps_and_bugs.title: Daemon start/stop/status are POSIX-oriented and degrade on Windows
gaps_and_bugs.detail: cmd_daemon_start uses start_new_session=True and os.kill(pid, 0)/SIGTERM (commands/platform.py:262,306,325). On Windows start_new_session is ignored and os.kill semantics differ; signal.SIGTERM kill of a detached python may not cleanly stop the daemon. No Windows-specific handling though the documented dev env is Windows 11.

gaps_and_bugs.title: `session show --log` / resume rely on agent_log/due_for_resume without guarding missing methods
gaps_and_bugs.detail: cmd_session_show calls orch.agent_log(...) and cmd_session_start (watch) calls orch.due_for_resume/resume_session unconditionally; if a driver (e.g. headless) doesn't implement these the command raises AttributeError rather than a friendly message. No try/except around the multiplexer RPC in the watch loop except KeyboardInterrupt.

gaps_and_bugs.title: Scope-listed `commands/common.py` does not exist
gaps_and_bugs.detail: The task scope and _EXCLUDED_MODULES reserve the name 'common', but there is no commands/common.py. Shared CLI helpers (confirm, api-key gate, query-filter parsing) live in main.py and are injected as kwargs into each module's impl — so a future common.py is the intended home but is currently unimplemented; helpers are centralized in main.py instead.

improvement_ideas.title: Extract shared CLI helpers into commands/common.py
improvement_ideas.rationale: main.py is a thin dispatcher except it also owns _confirm_action, _require_api_key, _parse_query_filters, _filter_proficiency_data_by_org, _get_psm/_get_orchestrator/_get_platform_home, all passed as kwargs. The name 'common' is already excluded from discovery — moving these there shrinks main.py to pure wiring and removes the vestigial _PROVIDER_KEY_MAPPING/_resolve_llm_provider dead code at the same time.

improvement_ideas.title: Add a public records accessor to OrchestrationPatternStore and stop touching _records
improvement_ideas.rationale: Two CLI handlers read store._records; a public list_records()/iter_records() would decouple the CLI from store internals and prevent silent empty-history regressions.

improvement_ideas.title: Validate org.target_repo_path before analyze/apply instead of silently falling back to '.'
improvement_ideas.rationale: The Path('.') fallback in cmd_analyze and cmd_proposal_apply can target the wrong directory. A guard that errors with a remediation hint when target_repo_path is unset/missing prevents accidental modification of the Pantheon repo itself.

improvement_ideas.title: Generate shell completion + a top-level `pantheon status` alias
improvement_ideas.rationale: The CLI has deep nested subcommands (org/proposal/platform/daemon/session/orchestration/agent/goal). argcomplete-based completion and a short alias for `platform status` would materially improve discoverability noted in the epilog quick-start.

improvement_ideas.title: Add a /triage-style CLI smoke test that asserts build_parser registers every handler_name in HANDLERS
improvement_ideas.rationale: Auto-discovery + a hand-maintained HANDLERS dict in main.py can drift (a registered subcommand with no HANDLERS entry only fails at runtime via parser.error). A test walking the parse tree and cross-checking handler_name against HANDLERS catches this at CI time.

improvement_ideas.title: Make daemon lifecycle cross-platform (psutil-based) or document Windows unsupported
improvement_ideas.rationale: Given the documented Windows-first dev env, the os.kill/SIGTERM/start_new_session daemon code is fragile; using psutil for process liveness/termination (or a Windows job-object) would make daemon start/stop/status reliable.

improvement_ideas.title: Unify the analyze->approve flow behind `pantheon proposal apply` and make `approve` a documented thin alias
improvement_ideas.rationale: cmd_approve simply calls cmd_proposal_apply with identical args; the two parsers duplicate github-repo/github-token/yes options. Consolidating reduces drift and clarifies that approve == proposal apply.

improvement_ideas.title: Add `pantheon session attach`/`logs --follow` for live agent output
improvement_ideas.rationale: session show --log only tails a fixed 4000 chars once; a follow mode (streaming the multiplexer surface output) would make multi-agent sessions usable for monitoring long claude runs alongside the existing --watch status loop.

components.name: AbstractGoalPipeline
components.role: Top-level orchestrator that chains the 5 goal-lifecycle stages (parse -> decompose -> instantiate -> execute -> verify) and returns a PipelineResult

components.name: GoalParser
components.role: M-01: converts natural-language goal text into a StructuredGoal (type/scope/scale/domain/criteria/constraints) via rule-based keyword matching, with an optional LLM path

components.name: GoalDecomposer
components.role: M-02: expands a StructuredGoal into an Epic/Story/Task tree (GoalPlan) using per-goal-type templates; flags task executability against CapabilityRegistry skills

components.name: OrgInstantiator
components.role: M-03: picks/creates the Organization for a goal — reuses an existing org if its purpose matches, else designs a new one via OrganizationDesigner; does NOT persist it

components.name: ExecutionCoordinator
components.role: M-04: topologically sorts the plan's tasks, skips ineligible/failed-dependency tasks, and 'executes' each via an optional PreTaskOrchestrator with retry; tracks ExecutionProgress

components.name: GoalVerifier
components.role: M-05: scores goal achievement (0-100%) by rule-checking success criteria against ExecutionProgress, blends in task-completion rate, and emits recommendations; 70% threshold = achieved

components.name: GoalLibrary
components.role: M-06: JSON-backed store of achieved-goal templates at ~/.pantheon/goal_library.json for reuse; only read path (status) is wired in production

components.name: GoalScheduler
components.role: M-08: in-memory queue/cap for tracking multiple concurrent goal executions; currently referenced only by tests (dead in production)

components.name: goals __init__
components.role: Empty package marker (1 blank line) — no re-exports, callers import submodules directly

entrypoints.name: pantheon goal run <goal_text>
entrypoints.summary: Async handler cmd_goal_run in commands/goal.py: gates on require_api_key (claude CLI), constructs AbstractGoalPipeline(), runs it, prints result.summary(). Does NOT persist org or goal history.

entrypoints.name: pantheon goal status

entrypoints.summary: api_goals_stream: SSE wrapper around _perform_goal_run emitting start/progress/result/done/error events. Progress messages are cosmetic (fixed 'Planning...'/'Saving...') — not driven by real ExecutionProgress.

entrypoints.name: chat tool run_goal / slash /goal
entrypoints.summary: _tool_run_goal in agents/chat_agent.py runs AbstractGoalPipeline and returns its summary; exposed both as a Claude tool ('run_goal') and as the /goal <text> no-API slash command.

flows_participated.role_in_flow: This subsystem IS the flow: natural-language text -> StructuredGoal -> GoalPlan(Epic/Story/Task) -> Organization -> autonomous task execution -> achievement verification + recommendations, surfaced as PipelineResult.summary().
commands/goal.py is the CLI front door; goal run drives the full pipeline, goal status reads the (currently never-populated) GoalLibrary.
POST /api/goals/run + /api/goals/stream drive the pipeline from the React frontend; results persist to goal_history.json and broadcast over /ws, then appear in execution-history and global search.
ChatAgent exposes the pipeline as the run_goal tool and the /goal slash command, letting conversational requests trigger autonomous goal execution without an API key.

OrgInstantiator (M-03) bridges goals into the org subsystem by invoking core/hierarchy/org_designer.OrganizationDesigner.design()/instantiate(); however the produced Organization is never saved to platform state, so it does not actually register.

ExecutionCoordinator is designed to delegate each task to PreTaskOrchestrator.analyze() for pattern/agent selection — but no production caller injects an orchestrator, so execution is effectively a stubbed pass-through.

state_touched: ~/.pantheon/goal_library.json (GoalLibrary — written only by the never-invoked save_achieved_goal; read by `pantheon goal status`)
In-memory only: GoalScheduler._executions, ExecutionProgress, PipelineResult (none persisted by the pipeline itself)

gaps_and_bugs.title: Pipeline never executes real work — ExecutionCoordinator is a no-op stub
gaps_and_bugs.detail: AbstractGoalPipeline.__init__ creates ExecutionCoordinator(pre_task_orchestrator=pre_task_orchestrator), and every production caller (commands/goal.py, web/server.py _perform_goal_run, agents/chat_agent.py _tool_run_goal) constructs AbstractGoalPipeline() with no orchestrator. So in ExecutionCoordinator._execute_task the `if self._orchestrator:` branch is never taken; tasks immediately return result_summary='直接実行（オーケストレーターなし）' and are marked DONE. No agent ever runs, no code is written, no claude CLI is invoked — yet GoalVerifier then reports ~100% achievement. The whole pipeline is a convincing simulation.

gaps_and_bugs.title: Instantiated Organization is never persisted
gaps_and_bugs.detail: OrgInstantiator.instantiate() designs/creates an Organization (and its docstring/AGENTS notes say 'Platform State への保存は呼び出し元が担当'), but no caller saves it. commands/goal.py, _perform_goal_run, and _tool_run_goal all discard org_result.organization after reading .name. So `pantheon goal run` claims to create a 'new' org that never appears in `pantheon org list` / GET /api/organizations.

gaps_and_bugs.title: Existing-org reuse path can never fire from production callers
gaps_and_bugs.detail: OrgInstantiator._find_reusable_org only searches self._existing_orgs, but every production caller instantiates OrgInstantiator() with no existing_orgs (the list defaults to []). The reuse branch is exercised only in tests. As a result every goal run reports is_new=True and would (if persistence existed) spawn a duplicate org per run.

gaps_and_bugs.title: GoalLibrary write path and GoalScheduler are dead code in production
gaps_and_bugs.detail: GoalLibrary.save_achieved_goal/record_use/find_similar are never called outside tests, so `pantheon goal status` always shows 'no history' even after successful runs. GoalScheduler (M-08) is referenced only by tests — the web/CLI run goals synchronously with no scheduling, queueing, or max_parallel enforcement.

gaps_and_bugs.title: SSE stream progress is fabricated, not real ExecutionProgress
gaps_and_bugs.detail: POST /api/goals/stream emits fixed 'Planning goal execution...' / 'Saving goal history...' messages and only yields the final result; ExecutionCoordinator already supports a progress_callback, but the pipeline never wires one through, so the UI cannot show per-task progress despite the data existing.

gaps_and_bugs.title: Verifier's success-criterion checks are keyword heuristics with no ground truth
gaps_and_bugs.detail: GoalVerifier._check_criterion judges criteria like 'テストがパスする' purely from whether DONE tasks contain the word 'テスト' in their title and from task-completion %, never running tests, coverage, or security tools. Combined with the no-op coordinator, overall_achieved is essentially always True regardless of real outcome.

gaps_and_bugs.title: LLM JSON extraction regex is non-greedy and breaks on nested objects
gaps_and_bugs.detail: Both GoalParser._parse_with_llm and GoalDecomposer._decompose_with_llm use re.search(r'\{.*?\}', content, re.DOTALL) to grab the JSON. The non-greedy `.*?` stops at the first closing brace, so any nested object (e.g. the decomposer's epics/stories/tasks structure) is truncated and json.loads fails, silently falling back to heuristics/templates. The LLM path is effectively unreachable for decomposition.

gaps_and_bugs.title: use_llm plumbed through but parser/decomposer/verifier have no llm_client
gaps_and_bugs.detail: AbstractGoalPipeline.run(use_llm=...) forwards the flag, but the default GoalParser()/GoalDecomposer()/GoalVerifier() are constructed with llm_client=None, so `use_llm=True` is a silent no-op everywhere. There is also no integration with the project's actual generation backend (core/runtime/claude_code.ClaudeCodeProvider) — the llm_client interface expects a hosted-LLM `.invoke()`, which contradicts the no-API-key architecture.

gaps_and_bugs.title: Heuristic classifier ties broken by template order, not relevance
gaps_and_bugs.detail: GoalParser._parse_heuristic picks the first rule with the strictly-greatest keyword match count; on ties the earlier rule in _CLASSIFICATION_RULES wins. NEW_SERVICE keywords include broad terms ('API','システム','新しい') placed late, while IMPROVEMENT is last, so mixed goals (e.g. 'improve the API') can misclassify in ways sensitive to list ordering rather than intent.

improvement_ideas.title: Wire a real execution backend into ExecutionCoordinator
improvement_ideas.rationale: Inject PreTaskOrchestrator (and ultimately BaseAgent runs via ClaudeCodeProvider) into AbstractGoalPipeline so tasks actually run instead of returning a stub summary. This is the single change that turns the pipeline from a simulation into a working autonomous executor.

improvement_ideas.title: Persist instantiated Organizations and reuse-search them
improvement_ideas.rationale: Have the pipeline (or its callers) call psm.save_organization(org_result.organization) on is_new and populate OrgInstantiator(existing_orgs=psm.list_organizations()) so reuse works. Without this, goals neither create durable orgs nor avoid duplicates.

improvement_ideas.title: Close the learning loop with GoalLibrary on success
improvement_ideas.rationale: Call GoalLibrary.save_achieved_goal()/record_use() at the end of a successful run and have GoalDecomposer.find_similar() seed plans from past templates. This activates M-06's intended self-improvement and makes `pantheon goal status` meaningful.

improvement_ideas.title: Stream true ExecutionProgress over SSE/WS
improvement_ideas.rationale: Pass a progress_callback into ExecutionCoordinator that pushes ExecutionProgress.to_dict() through _updates_hub / the SSE generator, replacing the fabricated two-line progress with real per-task status for the React UI.

improvement_ideas.title: Replace keyword verification with evidence-based checks
improvement_ideas.rationale: Let GoalVerifier optionally run pytest/coverage/ruff/security tools (via the existing runtime) and feed real artifacts into _check_criterion, so achievement_pct reflects reality rather than DONE-task title keywords.

improvement_ideas.title: Activate GoalScheduler for concurrent goal runs
improvement_ideas.rationale: Back the web/CLI run path with GoalScheduler (max_parallel, active executions, status) so multiple long-running goals can be queued/observed; currently M-08 is dead and runs are blocking-synchronous.

improvement_ideas.title: Add a /goal-pipeline CC command or skill documenting the lifecycle
improvement_ideas.rationale: Mirror the existing /add-cli-command pattern with a helper that scaffolds new goal-type templates in GoalDecomposer._TEMPLATES + GOAL_TYPE_TO_TEMPLATE + org_designer template, keeping the three parallel maps in sync (they are easy to desync today).

improvement_ideas.title: Robust JSON parsing for LLM paths and real use_llm wiring
improvement_ideas.rationale: Swap the non-greedy brace regex for a balanced-brace/json-decoder scan and inject a real llm_client (ClaudeCodeProvider-backed) so use_llm=True actually improves parsing/decomposition instead of silently falling back.

improvement_ideas.title: Centralize goal lifecycle state under <repo>/.pantheon
improvement_ideas.rationale: Goal runs are inherently repo-scoped (they target a codebase) yet history/library live only in global ~/.pantheon and the pipeline never records which repo it ran against. Persisting StructuredGoal/GoalPlan/ExecutionProgress per-repo would enable resumption, audit, and re-verification.

components.name: GUI settings store
components.role: Reads/writes ~/.pantheon/gui_settings.json (LLM provider/model, masked legacy API keys, daemon defaults, model_configurations, prompt_templates, policy_rules). Vestigial provider-key surface only — generation never flows through it.

components.name: Frontend API client
components.role: React 19 frontend's typed fetch wrapper that consumes these /api routes and /ws sockets (the primary consumer of this subsystem).

flows_participated.role_in_flow: POST /api/init bootstraps the platform + Meta-Improvement Org; POST /api/welcome seeds a sample org; GET /api/platform/status drives the dashboard's initialized/has_llm gating.
POST/GET/PUT/DELETE /api/organizations and the icon endpoints are the full CRUD surface backing the Orgs page; create goes through bootstrap_platform + create_default_organization.

Owns the entire web path: /api/analyze(+/stream) runs CodeReviewAgent and persists proposals; /api/organizations/{org}/proposals lists them; /api/proposals/{org}/{id}/approve runs OrchestratorAgent to apply (branch/PR), reject/batch round it out; events stream over /ws/updates.
POST /api/goals/run(+/stream) is the HTTP front-door to AbstractGoalPipeline; /api/goals/history surfaces results on the Data page.
The /api/sessions/* family is the AI-operations dashboard control plane over SessionOrchestrator + wmux/cmux/headless drivers and the local claude CLI.
Provides both stateless (/api/chat), persisted-session (/api/chat/sessions/*), and live (/ws/chat) chat surfaces over agents.chat_agent + slash commands.
/api/daemon/start|stop|status spawn/kill the core._daemon_runner background loop; this is the web equivalent of `pantheon daemon`.
Serves the built React SPA (/, /{full_path}, /assets) and is the sole backend the frontend (web/frontend/src/lib/api.ts) talks to.
/api/tasks/* expose TaskQueue; /api/orchestration/analyze/{task_type} and /api/agents|skills expose the capability/orchestration layer.

state_touched: ~/.pantheon/gui_settings.json (GUI settings; masked legacy keys, daemon defaults, policy_rules)
web/dist/ (built SPA served; falls back to web/static)

gaps_and_bugs.title: SPA catch-all swallows unknown GET /api/* paths (no JSON 404)
gaps_and_bugs.detail: `@app.get("/{full_path:path}")` (web/server.py:2995) matches ANY unmatched GET, including a mistyped /api/whatever, and returns index.html with 200 instead of a 404. Real 404s only come from explicit HTTPException in handlers. A GET to a nonexistent /api route therefore returns HTML 200, which breaks API clients and can mask routing bugs. (POST/PUT/DELETE to unknown paths still 405/404 since the catch-all is GET-only.)

gaps_and_bugs.title: Long-running agent work runs inside async handlers on the event loop
gaps_and_bugs.detail: POST /api/analyze, /api/goals/run, and /api/proposals/{org}/{id}/approve `await` CodeReviewAgent / AbstractGoalPipeline / OrchestratorAgent.run() directly in the request coroutine. These invoke the claude CLI and can take minutes; sessions correctly use asyncio.to_thread but analyze/goal/approve do not, so any blocking subprocess/file I/O inside those agents stalls the whole event loop (and all /ws/updates clients). web-api.md explicitly requires non-blocking handlers.

gaps_and_bugs.title: No authentication/authorization on any endpoint; destructive + RCE-adjacent routes exposed
gaps_and_bugs.detail: Every route is unauthenticated. With host default 0.0.0.0 (run_server / cmd_serve), anyone on the network can DELETE orgs, start the daemon subprocess, start arbitrary multiplexed claude sessions with attacker-supplied prompts (POST /api/sessions, prompt up to 20000 chars), set org target_repo_path, and write knowledge files. CORS allows credentials with localhost origins by default but the API itself has no auth gate.

gaps_and_bugs.title: PUT /api/settings persists secrets to plaintext env vars and a world-readable-on-Windows file
gaps_and_bugs.detail: api_update_settings writes provider API keys into os.environ and gui_settings.json. _set_settings_file_permissions is a no-op on Windows (os.name=='nt' early return), so on the documented Windows dev env the file holding keys is not chmod-restricted. Keys are also vestigial (generation uses claude CLI), so storing them at all is dead-but-dangerous surface.

gaps_and_bugs.title: approve proposal can leave a proposal stuck 'in_progress' if the request is cancelled mid-apply
gaps_and_bugs.detail: _approve_proposal_internal sets status to 'in_progress' then awaits OrchestratorAgent.run(); only success->done and failure->failed are handled. If the client disconnects / request is cancelled (or the process dies) during the long agent run, the proposal stays 'in_progress' forever and is excluded from the pending list (is_active filter), with no resume/timeout path.

gaps_and_bugs.title: _has_llm ignores its settings argument and only checks the claude CLI
gaps_and_bugs.detail: _has_llm(settings) always returns claude_available() and never inspects the passed settings; the parameter is dead. Combined with the large vestigial provider-key block (_PROVIDER_KEY_MAPPING, FALLBACK_MODELS, get_settings masking 5 providers), there is substantial dead/misleading surface that suggests multi-provider support that does not exist.

gaps_and_bugs.title: daemon start/stop uses os.kill semantics that are unreliable on Windows
gaps_and_bugs.detail: _is_process_running uses os.kill(pid,0) and api_daemon_stop uses os.kill(pid, SIGTERM); on Windows os.kill(pid,0) raises for foreign PIDs and SIGTERM maps to TerminateProcess, so PID reuse can mis-report 'running' and the stop path is fragile on the documented Windows dev environment.

gaps_and_bugs.title: web/server.py is a ~3000-line god-module mixing routes, models, persistence, SSE, and SPA
gaps_and_bugs.detail: All Pydantic models, ~50 routes across 9 domains, file-backed stores, the WebSocket hub, and the SPA fallback live in one file. This makes the explicit-404 rule fragile and review/testing harder; there is no APIRouter decomposition.

improvement_ideas.title: Add an explicit /api 404 guard route ahead of the SPA catch-all
improvement_ideas.rationale: Insert a route (or middleware) that returns JSON 404 for any unmatched path starting with /api, so the SPA catch-all only serves client routes. This hardens the documented 'preserve explicit 404 handling' rule and stops HTML-200 responses to mistyped API calls; add a regression test in tests/test_web_server.py.

improvement_ideas.title: Offload agent-running handlers to a worker / background-task model
improvement_ideas.rationale: Wrap analyze/goal/approve agent calls in asyncio.to_thread (as sessions already do) or convert them to queued background tasks that report progress over /ws/updates, returning a job id immediately. Removes event-loop stalls and aligns all three with the existing streaming UX.

improvement_ideas.title: Split web/server.py into APIRouter modules by domain
improvement_ideas.rationale: Extract organizations/proposals/goals/sessions/chat/knowledge/daemon/settings into web/routers/*.py with shared deps in web/server.py. Shrinks the god-module, isolates the 404/SPA rule, and makes the fastapi-endpoint skill flow safer.

improvement_ideas.title: Add an optional auth token / loopback-only bind for serve
improvement_ideas.rationale: Gate mutating + session/daemon endpoints behind a token (env or gui_settings) and default cmd_serve --host to 127.0.0.1. Closes the unauthenticated-RCE-adjacent exposure (arbitrary claude session prompts, org deletion, daemon spawn) on shared networks.

improvement_ideas.title: Reaper/timeout for stuck 'in_progress' proposals and crashed daemon/sessions
improvement_ideas.rationale: Add a sweep (on status fetch or a small background loop) that re-opens proposals stuck 'in_progress' past a timeout and reconciles daemon.pid/session records against live processes, so a cancelled approve or killed worker is self-healing rather than permanently wedged.

improvement_ideas.title: Remove the vestigial multi-provider key surface
improvement_ideas.rationale: Delete/quarantine _PROVIDER_KEY_MAPPING, FALLBACK_MODELS, the 5-provider masking in /api/settings, and make _has_llm drop its unused settings arg. Eliminates dead secret-handling code and the false impression of hosted-LLM support, matching the claude-CLI-only architecture.

improvement_ideas.title: Add an end-to-end web-flow integration test (analyze->approve over the API)
improvement_ideas.rationale: tests/test_web_server.py covers individual routes and 404s, but there is no test that exercises analyze -> list proposals -> approve with mocked agents plus /ws/updates broadcasts. A flow test would lock in the proposal lifecycle and event contract the frontend depends on.

improvement_ideas.title: Make settings-file permission hardening real (or encrypt) on Windows
improvement_ideas.rationale: _set_settings_file_permissions no-ops on Windows. Use an ACL-based restriction (icacls) or OS keyring for any retained secrets so gui_settings.json isn't a plaintext key store on the primary dev platform.

components.name: CapabilityRegistry
components.role: Catalog of all known capabilities (agents/skills/tools) the system has, persisted to capability_registry.json; the 'do I already have this?' lookup.

components.name: CapabilityGapAnalyzer
components.role: Compares repeated operation patterns against current capabilities to detect missing agents/skills/tools; zero-token heuristic mode plus optional LLM mode; persists capability_gaps.json.

components.name: OperationPatternDetector
components.role: Logs every agent/CLI operation to operation_log.jsonl and flags operation_types repeated >=3 times as RepeatedPattern; the input feed for gap analysis.

components.name: CodebaseIndexer
components.role: AST-based incremental (mtime-diff) codebase index saved to <repo>/.pantheon/codebase_index.json; extracts classes/module-fns/imports/docstring per file to cut token cost vs re-reading raw files.

components.name: CodebaseSnapshot
components.role: Turns the index into a purpose-ranked, token-budgeted text snapshot per mode (exploration/code_review/improvement/security/meta_improvement) for prompt injection.

components.name: AgentSkillEngine
components.role: Converts a SpecialistAgent's AgentSkill list into system-prompt persona/focus/output blocks (via SkillLoader YAML) and knowledge-search tags.

components.name: DependencyGraphBuilder
components.role: Standalone AST import-graph builder (file -> imported local modules) with reverse-dependents and circular-import detection; produces the JSON graph intended for visualization but currently only exercised by tests.

components.name: SkillGapDetector
components.role: Maps recurring failure categories (security/performance/testing/architecture) to a recommended skill once a category hits 3 failures; lighter-weight sibling of CapabilityGapAnalyzer.

components.name: SelfExtensionPipeline
components.role: Orchestrates a single CapabilityGap into an ImprovementProposal: ToolDesignAgent.design -> SelfCodeWriter.write_code -> SelfIntegrationTester.validate_syntax -> save proposal (HUMAN_REQUIRED), never auto-merging.

components.name: SelfIntegrationTester
components.role: Validates generated self-extension code (syntax / integration) before a proposal is created.

components.name: intelligence package facade
components.role: Re-exports the intelligence layer surface (indexer, snapshot, skill engine, registries, detectors); note its module list/docstring is stale vs current YAML-loader-based registration.

entrypoints.name: pantheon orchestration capabilities
entrypoints.summary: Prints CapabilityRegistry agents/skills plus unimplemented gaps from CapabilityGapAnalyzer. Handler cmd_orchestration_capabilities in commands/orchestration.py (registered as 'orchestration capabilities' subparser, wired via main.HANDLERS).

entrypoints.name: GET /api/orchestration/analyze/{task_type}
entrypoints.summary: Web GUI orchestration analysis: builds CapabilityRegistry.scan_and_register_all() then PreTaskOrchestrator.analyze to recommend agents/pattern for a task type (web/server.py ~line 2802).

entrypoints.name: CodebaseExplorerAgent.explore
entrypoints.summary: The production consumer of CodebaseIndexer+CodebaseSnapshot; builds/refreshes the per-repo index, generates a mode-specific snapshot, LLM-analyzes it, records 'codebase_scan' operations into OperationPatternDetector (agents/codebase_explorer_agent.py).

entrypoints.name: PreTaskOrchestrator (capability-aware routing)
entrypoints.summary: Consumes an injected CapabilityRegistry to list agents and feed TaskRouter; also records operations via OperationPatternDetector (core/orchestration/pre_task_orchestrator.py).

entrypoints.name: SelfExtensionPipeline.run_for_gap
entrypoints.summary: Gap -> design -> code -> validate -> ImprovementProposal(status=proposed, category=self_extension) awaiting human approval; the self-improvement entry of the intelligence layer.

flows_participated.role_in_flow: Core engine: OperationPatternDetector logs repeats -> CapabilityGapAnalyzer detects missing capability vs CapabilityRegistry -> SelfExtensionPipeline turns each gap into a human-gated ImprovementProposal (ToolDesignAgent/SelfCodeWriter/SelfIntegrationTester).

Feeds the proposal stage: self-extension gaps and skill gaps become ImprovementProposals saved via RepoStateManager, then flow into the existing approve/apply path; CodebaseExplorerAgent supplies cheap codebase context to reviewers.
CapabilityRegistry is the agent inventory PreTaskOrchestrator+TaskRouter route against; backs both the CLI 'orchestration analyze/capabilities' and GET /api/orchestration/analyze.
CodebaseIndexer + CodebaseSnapshot provide the index/snapshot that CodebaseExplorerAgent uses to replace raw-file reads, reducing tokens for review/exploration tasks.
Supplies recommended agents and capability data to the React frontend via the orchestration analyze endpoint.

gaps_and_bugs.title: DependencyGraphBuilder is not wired into any product surface (no Atlas viz consumer)
gaps_and_bugs.detail: core/intelligence/dependency_graph.py is referenced only by tests and as a suggested capability NAME in heuristic rules/self-extension fixtures. No CLI command, web endpoint, or agent calls build()/save_graph(), so the JSON graph it produces is never surfaced to a UI/Atlas. Separately, core/quality/meta_improvement_analyzer.py reimplements its own _detect_circular_imports instead of reusing this builder. If an Atlas dependency visualization is intended, an endpoint/CLI that calls DependencyGraphBuilder.build and serves the graph is missing.

gaps_and_bugs.title: Heuristic gap_id collisions from second-granularity timestamps
gaps_and_bugs.detail: CapabilityGapAnalyzer._analyze_heuristic builds gap_id=f'gap:{pattern.pattern_key}:{strftime("%Y%m%d%H%M%S")}'. Multiple rules matching the same pattern_key within one second (or two analyze() runs in the same second) generate identical gap_ids. mark_implemented(gap_id) iterates and stops at the first match, so duplicate-id gaps can never all be marked implemented, and saved gaps lose identity. Use uuid4 or include suggested_name/index in the id.

gaps_and_bugs.title: get_unused_capabilities threshold logic likely returns nearly everything
gaps_and_bugs.detail: In CapabilityRegistry.get_unused_capabilities, last_used defaults to cap.added_at when never used, and is_unused starts True whenever usage_count==0. The date math only ever ORs in more True. So any capability with usage_count==0 (the normal state right after scan_and_register_all) is reported as unused regardless of days_threshold, making it noisy for any deprecation workflow. The days vs days_threshold dual-parameter also makes the API confusing.

gaps_and_bugs.title: Stale documentation: registration source no longer AST/enum based
gaps_and_bugs.detail: capability_registry.py module docstring still says it scans agents/ Python classes and AgentSkill enum, but _scan_agents/_scan_skills now read AgentLoader (agents/definitions/*.yaml) and SkillLoader (skills/*.yaml). core/intelligence/__init__.py docstring and AGENTS.md likewise describe enum-based skills. agent_skill_engine.py still imports the AgentSkill enum yet pulls all content from YAML. This divergence misleads contributors about where to add capabilities.

gaps_and_bugs.title: CapabilityGapAnalyzer never auto-marks a gap implemented when the capability later exists
gaps_and_bugs.detail: _analyze_heuristic suppresses re-creating a gap if suggested_name is already a registry capability, but previously-saved gaps for a now-existing capability are never auto-flipped to implemented. get_all_gaps() keeps surfacing them until something calls mark_implemented explicitly, which nothing in the analyze loop does. Result: stale 'gaps' that are actually filled.

gaps_and_bugs.title: CodebaseIndexer only AST-parses Python; other supported extensions get empty structure
gaps_and_bugs.detail: SUPPORTED_EXTENSIONS includes ts/js/go/rs/java/rb/cpp/c, but _index_file only calls _parse_python for .py. For all other languages classes/functions/imports/docstring stay empty, so search() and snapshot ranking degrade to path/size signal only on polyglot repos. mtime-based incremental update is fine, but the index is effectively Python-only.

gaps_and_bugs.title: invalidate_cache name-based fallback can drop unrelated files
gaps_and_bugs.detail: codebase_indexer.invalidate_cache removes any indexed entry whose basename matches a target basename (any(Path(path).name == Path(rel_path).name ...)). In repos with duplicate filenames (e.g. multiple __init__.py or utils.py), invalidating one path silently evicts all same-named files from the index.

improvement_ideas.title: Add a dependency-graph endpoint + Atlas page
improvement_ideas.rationale: Wire DependencyGraphBuilder.build/detect_circular_imports into a GET /api/atlas/dependency-graph (and a 'pantheon atlas' CLI), caching the JSON in <repo>/.pantheon, so the React frontend can render the module graph and highlight cycles. Reuse it in meta_improvement_analyzer instead of its duplicate circular-import detector.

improvement_ideas.title: Close the self-extension loop: auto-resolve filled gaps
improvement_ideas.rationale: Have CapabilityGapAnalyzer.analyze() (or a periodic job) call mark_implemented for any saved gap whose suggested_name now exists in CapabilityRegistry, and have SelfExtensionPipeline mark the source gap implemented on successful proposal apply. Removes stale gap noise from CLI/web.

improvement_ideas.title: Make gap/capability IDs collision-proof and add a usage-recording hook
improvement_ideas.rationale: Switch heuristic gap_id to uuid4 (or include suggested_name), and call CapabilityRegistry.record_usage from PreTaskOrchestrator/TaskRouter when an agent/skill is actually selected so usage_count/last_used become meaningful and get_unused_capabilities can drive a real deprecation report.

improvement_ideas.title: Multi-language indexing via tree-sitter (or pluggable parsers)
improvement_ideas.rationale: Add per-language parsers behind _index_file so the ts/js/go/etc. extensions already in SUPPORTED_EXTENSIONS actually populate classes/functions/imports, making CodebaseSnapshot and search useful on the React frontend and polyglot targets, not just Python.

improvement_ideas.title: Schedule periodic gap analysis as a daemon/hook job
improvement_ideas.rationale: should_run_analysis/mark_analysis_run already implement a 7-day cadence but nothing calls them on a schedule. Add a platform run-all/daemon step (or Stop-hook) that runs OperationPatternDetector->CapabilityGapAnalyzer->SelfExtensionPipeline so gaps surface and become proposals without manual CLI invocation.

improvement_ideas.title: Fix stale docs and unify the skill source of truth
improvement_ideas.rationale: Update capability_registry.py/__init__.py/AGENTS.md docstrings to reflect YAML-based AgentLoader/SkillLoader registration, and decide whether AgentSkill enum stays the typed key or is generated from skills/*.yaml to avoid enum-vs-YAML drift that the registry and AgentSkillEngine straddle.

components.name: PreTaskOrchestrator
components.role: Pre-task meta-analysis engine: ANALYZE->RESEARCH->SELECT/SPAWN->EXECUTE->LEARN; picks an orchestration pattern + agents and runs them

components.name: TaskRouter / LoadBalancer
components.role: Scores registered agents against a weighted per-task-type skill profile (TASK_SKILL_REQUIREMENTS) and returns top-N agent ids; flags spawn when normalized score < 0.3

components.name: OrchestrationPatternStore
components.role: Persistent JSON library of PatternRecords; computes per task_type x pattern success-rate/quality stats and returns the best learned pattern (needs >=3 runs)

components.name: BestPracticeAdvisor
components.role: RESEARCH phase: pulls past insights from KnowledgeManager + pattern stats from the store, with static fallback best-practices, into research_notes

components.name: DynamicAgentSpawner
components.role: Creates or reuses a SpecialistAgent (fuzzy skill-alias resolution, min2/max3 skills) and registers it in CapabilityRegistry when no existing agent fits

components.name: GroupOrchestrator
components.role: Metrics-driven multi-org improvement: picks the highest-priority Organization, runs its improvement cycle through PreTaskOrchestrator, then ScoreUpdater updates autonomy/velocity

components.name: TaskQueue
components.role: JSON-file global task queue (~/.pantheon/task_queue.json) with file-lock, status lifecycle, priority ordering, and cleanup; backs the web dashboard

components.name: MultiOrgExecutor
components.role: Runs queued tasks concurrently (asyncio.Semaphore) against a caller-supplied executor_fn, updating TaskQueue status; supports cancellation

components.name: TaskMatcher
components.role: Proposal-category -> skill matcher (CATEGORY_SKILL_MAP) producing ranked agent dicts and a coverage match-rate; standalone, not wired into the live routing path

components.name: TeamCoordinator
components.role: In-memory per-task collaboration context so same-team agents can see each other's contributions and synthesize outputs; standalone, not wired into live execution

components.name: OrchestratorAgent (consumer)
components.role: BaseAgent facade the CLI uses; wraps PreTaskOrchestrator (analyze->execute) and exposes describe_routing for `pantheon orchestration analyze`

components.name: orchestration CLI handlers
components.role: Implements `pantheon orchestration {analyze,history,capabilities,self-review}` and `pantheon agent {list,status}` over the pattern store + capability registry

entrypoints.name: GET/POST /api/tasks, GET/DELETE /api/tasks/{id}

entrypoints.name: Orchestrator.run_meta_evolution_cycle
entrypoints.summary: core/orchestrator.py builds a GroupOrchestrator over all orgs and runs the metrics-driven smart improvement cycle

entrypoints.name: ExecutionCoordinator.execute (abstract-goal pipeline)
entrypoints.summary: Topologically sorts GoalPlan tasks and runs each through an injected PreTaskOrchestrator

flows_participated.role_in_flow: OrchestratorAgent routes `code_review` via PreTaskOrchestrator: TaskRouter scores agents on CODEBASE_EXPLORATION/PERFORMANCE_ANALYSIS/TOOL_INTEGRATION, pattern=REVIEW_LOOP, executes exec+reviewer; learned pattern can override default
ExecutionCoordinator drives GoalPlan tasks through an injected PreTaskOrchestrator, executing each task with the selected pattern/agents and skipping tasks whose dependencies failed

Orchestrator.run_meta_evolution_cycle -> GroupOrchestrator decides the highest-priority Organization by metrics, pre-analyzes a `meta_improvement` plan, runs the improvement graph, then ScoreUpdater updates autonomy/velocity
TaskQueue persists queued analyze/goal/improve/review tasks surfaced by /api/tasks; MultiOrgExecutor is the intended concurrent runner for pending tasks

PreTaskOrchestrator._record_execution writes PatternRecords to OrchestrationPatternStore + KnowledgeManager; analyze() later reads get_best_pattern to override the static profile, and self-review/history surface the stats

PreTaskOrchestrator marks spawn_new_agent + builds spawn_spec when routing finds no agent; DynamicAgentSpawner is meant to consume it to create/register a SpecialistAgent (currently the link is not invoked)

state_touched: ~/.pantheon (capability registry + knowledge insights written via PreTaskOrchestrator learning; KnowledgeManager.save_insight)
in-memory only: PreTaskOrchestrator._execution_log, DynamicAgentSpawner._spawned_agents, TeamCoordinator._contexts, LoadBalancer._loads, MultiOrgExecutor._running

gaps_and_bugs.title: PreTaskOrchestrator.batch_execute references asyncio without importing it
gaps_and_bugs.detail: core/orchestration/pre_task_orchestrator.py:278-303 uses asyncio.Semaphore and asyncio.gather, but the module has no top-level `import asyncio` (only local imports inside _execute_parallel at line 473 and _execute_sequential's `import copy`). Any call to batch_execute() raises NameError: name 'asyncio' is not defined. The public batch API is effectively dead/broken.

gaps_and_bugs.title: DynamicAgentSpawner is never invoked — spawn path is dead code
gaps_and_bugs.detail: PreTaskOrchestrator.analyze() sets spawn_new_agent and builds spawn_spec (via _build_spawn_spec), but execute()/the pattern executors never call DynamicAgentSpawner. Grep shows DynamicAgentSpawner(/SpawnRequest( are instantiated only in docs and tests, never in production. So 'create the agent if it doesn't exist' (the headline feature) does not actually happen; when no agent matches, _execute_single just returns AgentResult(success=False, 'No agent selected').

gaps_and_bugs.title: Learning loop is quality-blind: quality_score is always the 5.0 default
gaps_and_bugs.detail: _record_execution (pre_task_orchestrator.py:512) creates PatternRecord without quality_score/execution_time_ms, so every record stores quality_score=5.0. OrchestrationPatternStore.get_best_pattern and self-review's avg_quality<5.0 check therefore can never differentiate by quality; only success/fail truly drives learning, and self-review's quality criterion never fires.

gaps_and_bugs.title: GroupOrchestrator's internal PreTaskOrchestrator has no CapabilityRegistry
gaps_and_bugs.detail: core/orchestrator.py:115 does GroupOrchestrator(self.state) with no capability_registry/knowledge_manager/pattern_store. GroupOrchestrator.__init__ then builds a PreTaskOrchestrator with registry=None, so TaskRouter.route() always returns empty (fallback_used) and the printed 'recommended agents' is always '(default)'. Worse, with pattern_store=None the meta_improvement learning is NOT persisted across runs from this path.

gaps_and_bugs.title: REVIEW_LOOP has no loop / no quality gate
gaps_and_bugs.detail: _execute_review_loop (pre_task_orchestrator.py:448) runs the main agent once, optionally runs a reviewer once, and ignores the reviewer's result entirely (no re-run, no pass/fail gate). Despite the name and docstring ('品質不足なら再実行'), there is no iteration and the review output is discarded — the reviewer adds cost but cannot change the outcome.

gaps_and_bugs.title: PARALLEL_THEN_MERGE does not merge and double-instantiates agents
gaps_and_bugs.detail: _execute_parallel returns successful[0] with a comment '将来: マージロジック' — no actual merge. It also calls agent_factory(aid) twice per id (once in the comprehension condition, once for the value), doubling agent construction cost/side effects. TeamCoordinator.synthesize_outputs exists for exactly this but is never used here.

gaps_and_bugs.title: MultiOrgExecutor is never wired to the live task queue or web API
gaps_and_bugs.detail: TaskQueue is enqueued via POST /api/tasks, but nothing in web/server.py or the daemon calls MultiOrgExecutor.process_pending/run_parallel to actually drain pending tasks. Queued dashboard tasks stay PENDING forever unless an external caller supplies an executor_fn. The executor and the queue are connected only in tests.

gaps_and_bugs.title: TaskQueue uses POSIX fcntl flock that silently no-ops on Windows
gaps_and_bugs.detail: task_queue.py:49-61 imports fcntl for the cross-process file lock and swallows ImportError on Windows, falling back to only the in-process threading.RLock. Concurrent Pantheon processes on Windows (the documented dev/runtime platform) can corrupt task_queue.json since only the .tmp replace gives partial atomicity.

gaps_and_bugs.title: Dead/duplicate routing code and orphaned modules
gaps_and_bugs.detail: PreTaskOrchestrator._select_agents (lines 319-347) is never called — analyze() uses TaskRouter.route() instead, leaving two divergent scoring implementations. TaskMatcher (CATEGORY_SKILL_MAP) and TeamCoordinator are not referenced by any production caller (tests/docs only). TaskMatcher's skills (CODE_REVIEW, REFACTORING, TESTING) are not even members of the AgentSkill enum used elsewhere, so it can't interoperate with the real registry.

gaps_and_bugs.title: hierarchical / best_of_n patterns declared but unimplemented
gaps_and_bugs.detail: OrchestrationPattern defines HIERARCHICAL and BEST_OF_N (and meta_improvement's profile recommends HIERARCHICAL), but execute() has no branch for them and silently falls through to _execute_single. So the documented 'manager directs workers' pattern for self-improvement degrades to a single agent.

improvement_ideas.title: Wire DynamicAgentSpawner into PreTaskOrchestrator.execute
improvement_ideas.rationale: When analysis.spawn_new_agent is true, call DynamicAgentSpawner.spawn(SpawnRequest(spawn_spec)) and feed the new agent id back into the chosen pattern, then register it. This is the missing link that makes the advertised 'create the capability if it doesn't exist' loop actually run end-to-end.

improvement_ideas.title: Fix and test batch_execute (add top-level `import asyncio`)
improvement_ideas.rationale: Add module-level `import asyncio` and a unit test calling batch_execute; remove the redundant local imports. One-line fix that turns a NameError-on-call public API into a working parallel analysis entrypoint.

improvement_ideas.title: Feed real quality/timing into PatternRecord
improvement_ideas.rationale: Derive quality_score (e.g. from AgentResult quality fields or review outcome) and execution_time_ms in _record_execution so get_best_pattern and self-review's avg_quality gate become meaningful; today learning is success/fail-only and the quality threshold is unreachable.

improvement_ideas.title: Add a queue-draining worker that bridges TaskQueue -> MultiOrgExecutor -> OrchestratorAgent
improvement_ideas.rationale: A daemon/background task (or a /api/tasks/process endpoint) that pulls get_pending_tasks and runs them via MultiOrgExecutor.process_pending with an executor_fn built on OrchestratorAgent would make dashboard-queued tasks actually execute instead of sitting PENDING.

improvement_ideas.title: Implement real REVIEW_LOOP iteration and PARALLEL merge
improvement_ideas.rationale: Make _execute_review_loop honor the reviewer's verdict and re-run up to N times on failure, and have _execute_parallel use TeamCoordinator.synthesize_outputs (or a best_of_n scorer) instead of returning successful[0]; also stop calling agent_factory twice per id.

improvement_ideas.title: Pass CapabilityRegistry/pattern_store into GroupOrchestrator
improvement_ideas.rationale: core/orchestrator.py should construct GroupOrchestrator with a real CapabilityRegistry, KnowledgeManager and OrchestrationPatternStore so meta-evolution routing actually selects agents and persists learning across daemon runs.

improvement_ideas.title: Consolidate the two agent-scoring implementations
improvement_ideas.rationale: Delete PreTaskOrchestrator._select_agents (dead) and route everything through TaskRouter; either retire TaskMatcher/TeamCoordinator or integrate them, and reconcile TaskMatcher's CATEGORY_SKILL_MAP with the real AgentSkill enum so category-based matching is usable.

improvement_ideas.title: Cross-platform TaskQueue locking
improvement_ideas.rationale: Replace the fcntl-only lock with portalocker/msvcrt or an atomic-write+retry scheme so concurrent processes on Windows (the stated runtime) don't corrupt task_queue.json; add a multiprocess stress test.

improvement_ideas.title: Add a /triage-orchestration command/skill surfacing learned vs static patterns
improvement_ideas.rationale: A helper that diffs the static TASK_ORCHESTRATION_PROFILES against OrchestrationPatternStore.get_best_pattern per task_type would make pattern drift and unimplemented patterns (HIERARCHICAL/BEST_OF_N) visible, and guide where execute() branches are missing.
