gaps_and_bugs.title: Web GUI apply path can never create a GitHub PR (missing github_repo)
gaps_and_bugs.detail: web/server.py (~line 2433-2441) builds the improvement_execution AgentTask with input={repo_path, suggestion, github_token: os.getenv('GITHUB_TOKEN')} but NEVER passes github_repo. ImprovementExecutorAgent.run requires BOTH github_token AND github_repo to call create_improvement_pr, so the GUI always falls through to a local change. The CLI (commands/org.py) passes both, so PR creation only works from the CLI. The org has target_repo_path but no stored owner/repo identifier to supply here.

gaps_and_bugs.title: PersonaLoader / config.personas system is orphaned (dead in production)
gaps_and_bugs.detail: core/intelligence/persona_loader.PersonaLoader and config.settings.load_persona_configs are defined, exported, and validated, but no agent/runtime/web code instantiates them — only tests do. Specialist-agent personas actually come from skills/*.yaml via SkillLoader/agent_loader.build_system_prompt. The config/personas/*.yaml files (ceo, security_expert, strategic_analyst) and their allowed_tools/forbidden_actions (e.g. CEO 'no direct commit to main') are never enforced. This is misleading wiring that suggests a guardrail that does not exist.

gaps_and_bugs.title: create_improvement_pr does no path traversal / single-file limitation and uses PyGithub content as str without base64
gaps_and_bugs.detail: create_improvement_pr only updates/creates ONE file per proposal (proposal.file_path); multi-file refactors are impossible. It also passes raw str content to repo.update_file/create_file (PyGithub auto-encodes, OK) but the unused repo_path param and the catch-all 'except GithubException -> create_file' will mask real errors (e.g. permission/branch errors) by attempting a create. No retry/cleanup of the orphan branch if create_pull fails.

gaps_and_bugs.title: PR body contains a placeholder/broken link
gaps_and_bugs.detail: _build_pr_body emits 'このPRは [Pantheon](https://github.com/) によって自動生成されました' — the URL is the bare github.com homepage placeholder, not the actual Pantheon repo. Cosmetic but ships in every auto-PR.

gaps_and_bugs.title: Repo-reader scans are O(repo) with no caching and double rglob
gaps_and_bugs.detail: get_important_files calls repo_path.rglob('*') and then read_file_content for each candidate; get_file_tree separately rglob's the whole tree sorted. On large repos this is slow and re-walks the tree; results feed code-review prompts but there's no .gitignore awareness beyond the hardcoded EXCLUDE_DIRS set (e.g. won't honor a custom ignore).

gaps_and_bugs.title: Quality scripts and git pre-commit hook assume POSIX shell + python3 on PATH
gaps_and_bugs.detail: scripts/hooks/pre-commit is /bin/sh and calls 'python3 scripts/check_test_coverage.py'; install_hooks.sh is bash with chmod +x. On the documented Windows dev env (python not on PATH, no bash) the installed git hook will not run, so the page->test coverage gate is silently inert there. The .claude Node hooks are the only cross-platform automation.

gaps_and_bugs.title: validate_config.py is not wired into any automated gate
gaps_and_bugs.detail: scripts/validate_config.py is a solid validator (schema_version==1.0, capability_id 'agent:' prefix, skills 2-3, persona keys) but nothing invokes it — neither the git pre-commit hook nor a .claude hook nor CI. Drift between skills/*.yaml, the AgentSkill enum, and agents/definitions/*.yaml can land uncaught.

improvement_ideas.title: Fix web PR creation by persisting owner/repo on the Organization and passing github_repo
improvement_ideas.rationale: Store a github_repo (owner/repo) field alongside target_repo_path (derive from git remote on register) and pass it in the web/server.py apply task so the GUI reaches feature parity with the CLI for PR creation.

improvement_ideas.title: Either wire PersonaLoader into agent construction or delete it
improvement_ideas.rationale: config/personas/* currently looks like a governance layer (CEO forbidden from committing to main) but is inert. Either inject get_system_prompt_addon into agent_factory/agent_loader for matching roles, or remove the loader+configs to avoid a false sense of enforced guardrails.

improvement_ideas.title: Add a Pantheon CC hook/command that runs scripts/validate_config.py on YAML edits
improvement_ideas.rationale: A PostToolUse(Write|Edit) matcher on skills/*.yaml, config/**/*.yaml, and agents/definitions/*.yaml that runs validate_config.py would catch schema/enum drift immediately, closing the gap left by the inert git pre-commit on Windows.

improvement_ideas.title: Support multi-file PRs and a Git-Tree commit in pr_creator
improvement_ideas.rationale: Use PyGithub's create_git_tree/create_git_commit to apply all files of a proposal atomically in one branch/PR, and replace the GithubException catch-all with explicit handling + orphan-branch cleanup on failure.

improvement_ideas.title: Add a github-integration CC skill/subagent
improvement_ideas.rationale: Encapsulate the PyGithub PR contract (branch naming pantheon/improvement-<slug>, token/repo handling, _build_pr_body) so changes to PR creation are made consistently and tested against tests/test_pdca_rounds_71_80.py.

improvement_ideas.title: Make repo_reader gitignore-aware and cache the file tree
improvement_ideas.rationale: Honor .gitignore (e.g. via pathspec) and compute the tree once, sharing it between get_file_tree and get_important_files to cut redundant rglob walks on large target repos during analyze.

improvement_ideas.title: Port scripts/hooks/pre-commit and install_hooks.sh to a cross-platform installer
improvement_ideas.rationale: Provide a Node or pure-Python pre-commit so the page->test/help-coverage gate actually fires on the Windows dev env, matching the rest of the cross-platform .claude/ Node tooling.

improvement_ideas.title: Fix the placeholder PR body URL and stamp the proposal id/priority links
improvement_ideas.rationale: Point the auto-PR footer at the real Pantheon repo and include the proposal id so PRs are traceable back to <repo>/.pantheon proposal records.

components.name: SelfImprovementLoop
components.role: Lightweight per-org improvement cycle: pulls pending proposals from .pantheon, prioritizes, and dispatches to ImprovementExecutorAgent. This is what `pantheon platform run-all` actually runs.

components.name: SelfImprovementGraph (LangGraph)
components.role: Stateful LangGraph workflow with SQLite checkpointing and a real interrupt-based human-in-the-loop gate; richer alternative to SelfImprovementLoop that also runs strict review per executed proposal.

components.name: InternalConsultant (strict quality reviewer)
components.role: McKinsey/BCG-style strict reviewer that scores work across 6 dimensions via the claude CLI; health-aware strictness; emits QualityReview + auto ImprovementProposals.

components.name: WorkerTaskExecutor
components.role: Wraps any worker task with execute -> strict review -> proposal generation -> persist to .pantheon, automating the quality loop after each task.

components.name: Quality review trigger
components.role: Standalone helper to run a task then auto-fire strict review and proposal generation; re-exports generate_improvement_proposals_from_review.

components.name: SelfImprovementCycle (core self-improvement)
components.role: Human-approved core self-improvement: runs architecture meta-analysis, bumps core_version.json with HUMAN_REQUIRED marker, appends to core_improvement_history.jsonl.

components.name: MetaImprovementAnalyzer
components.role: AST-based heuristic architecture analyzer for the Pantheon core repo (large files >500 lines, complex modules >20 funcs, circular import hints); generates architecture-category meta proposals.

components.name: PromptEvolutionEngine
components.role: A/B prompt experiment store (prompt_experiments.json) and naive prompt evolution from quality history; selects best variant after >=3 uses.

components.name: ConfigAutoTuner
components.role: Recommends config/default.yaml threshold tweaks (low_health_threshold, review_cycles) from health/acceptance trends. Built but not wired to any caller.

components.name: TemplatePromoter
components.role: Promotes high-autonomy org structures to reusable YAML templates under ~/.pantheon/org_templates. Built but not wired.

components.name: error_handling decorator
components.role: Async retry/fallback decorator intended for LangGraph nodes so the graph never hard-stops. Currently unused (example-only).

components.name: PolicyEngine (HITL approval router)
components.role: Central rule engine routing every proposal to AUTO_APPROVE / HUMAN_REQUIRED / REJECT; loads ~/.pantheon/policy.yaml or DEFAULT_POLICY. Priority: reject > human_required > auto_approve > default(human).

components.name: PolicyOptimizer
components.role: Self-tunes policy rules from historical approve/reject decisions (adds reject rules on >=3 rejects, suggests loosening when >80% auto-approved). Built but not wired.

components.name: balanced_growth metrics
components.role: Primary metrics path actually used by CLI/web/orchestrator: per-org health (autonomy 0.4 + velocity 0.3 + avg_review 0.3), group balance (variance-based), improvement priority score.

components.name: ScoreUpdater
components.role: EMA-smoothed updater of org autonomy_score & improvement_velocity from execution outcomes (success/failure, suggestions, self-initiated, knowledge bonus); persists via state_manager.

components.name: HealthCalculator
components.role: Alternative real-time health score from proposals+decisions (accepted_ratio, pending penalty, 7-day recency bonus); renders CLI bar with lifecycle stage. Separate formula from balanced_growth.

components.name: OrganizationLifecycle
components.role: Maps autonomy_score to INCUBATING/GROWING/MATURE/AUTONOMOUS stages and derives auto-approve bonus + review intensity per stage (the intended lever connecting metrics->policy strictness).

components.name: Growth/learning/coevolution trackers
components.role: JSONL time-series recorders + trend/forecast: growth_history (linear regression predict_score), learning_curve (sparkline + knowledge/quality correlation), coevolution_graph (org vs developer-approval), milestones, velocity, group_balance. Mostly test-only.

entrypoints.name: pantheon platform run-all
entrypoints.summary: cmd_platform_run_all (commands/platform.py) scores orgs via get_improvement_priority_score, then runs SelfImprovementLoop.run_improvement_cycle per org in priority order.

entrypoints.name: pantheon platform status
entrypoints.summary: cmd_platform_status renders per-org health bars/badges (HEALTHY/WATCH/CRITICAL) using calculate_organization_metrics.

entrypoints.name: Autonomous scheduler (AutonomousScheduler)
entrypoints.summary: core/scheduler.py: EventDetector -> CodeReviewAgent -> PolicyEngine.evaluate routes each proposal to auto-apply / human-pending / reject. The ONLY runtime user of PolicyEngine.

entrypoints.name: GET /api/organizations and /api/organizations/{name}
entrypoints.summary: Returns org list/detail with health_score computed by calculate_organization_metrics.

entrypoints.name: POST /api/proposals/{org}/{id}/approve|reject (+ /batch)
entrypoints.summary: Manual human approval path; approve runs OrchestratorAgent to apply + branch/PR. NOTE: bypasses PolicyEngine entirely.

entrypoints.name: GET/POST /api/settings (policy_rules)
entrypoints.summary: Settings surface seeds/echoes policy_rules from DEFAULT_POLICY; stored in settings but not re-loaded into a live PolicyEngine.

entrypoints.name: run_improvement_for_organization (LangGraph HITL)
entrypoints.summary: Builds the SQLite-checkpointed graph with interrupt_before=['wait_for_human_approval']; resumed via request_human_approval_and_resume. Exercised by tests, not exposed via CLI/web.

flows_participated.role_in_flow: PolicyEngine (core/policy/engine.py) is the routing brain that classifies each ImprovementProposal as auto-approve/human-required/reject. InternalConsultant + trigger/worker_executor generate proposals from strict reviews. NOTE: the web manual approve/reject flow does NOT call PolicyEngine — only the autonomous scheduler does.

SelfImprovementLoop is the executed implementation (via `platform run-all`); SelfImprovementGraph is the richer LangGraph variant with persisted checkpoints and a HITL interrupt gate plus per-proposal strict review.

SelfImprovementCycle + MetaImprovementAnalyzer analyze the Pantheon core repo's architecture and emit HUMAN_REQUIRED meta-proposals, bumping core_version.json — the loop by which the platform improves itself.
balanced_growth.get_improvement_priority_score orders orgs for run-all; AutonomousScheduler embeds PolicyEngine for the event-driven auto-apply loop.
calculate_organization_metrics / calculate_group_metrics feed org health_score, group health and balance shown in the dashboard and org pages.
ScoreUpdater consumes ExecutionOutcome from agent runs to EMA-update autonomy_score & improvement_velocity, feeding back into lifecycle stage and metrics.

state_touched: <repo>/.pantheon/ proposals + quality reviews (RepoStateManager save_quality_review / save_improvement_proposal / update_proposal_status)
config/default.yaml (self_improvement.review_strictness/improvement_cycle thresholds read by InternalConsultant + ConfigAutoTuner)

gaps_and_bugs.title: Web manual approve/reject bypasses PolicyEngine entirely
gaps_and_bugs.detail: engine.py docstring claims 'human-origin and AI-origin both always pass through this engine,' but the only runtime caller of PolicyEngine.evaluate is core/scheduler.py (autonomous daemon). The web flow web/server.py:_approve_proposal_internal/_reject_proposal_internal applies proposals directly via OrchestratorAgent without ever consulting PolicyEngine, so reject/human-required rules are silently unenforced for GUI/CLI-driven approvals.

gaps_and_bugs.title: policy_rules saved in web settings are never loaded by PolicyEngine
gaps_and_bugs.detail: web/server.py persists policy_rules into settings (default DEFAULT_POLICY), but PolicyEngine loads only from ~/.pantheon/policy.yaml. Editing policy in the GUI has no effect on the scheduler's routing — the customized rules are write-only state.

gaps_and_bugs.title: Two divergent, uncoordinated health-score formulas
gaps_and_bugs.detail: balanced_growth.calculate_organization_metrics (autonomy*0.4+velocity*0.3+review*0.3) is used by CLI/web, while HealthCalculator.calculate (50 + accepted_ratio*30 - pending_penalty + recency) is a completely different formula used by nothing in production. The same 'health_score' concept yields different numbers depending on path, and HealthCalculator's lifecycle-stage rendering is never surfaced to users.

gaps_and_bugs.title: Lifecycle->policy strictness lever is dead code
gaps_and_bugs.detail: OrganizationLifecycle.get_auto_approve_bonus / get_review_intensity are designed to make AUTONOMOUS orgs auto-approve more and review lighter, but nothing calls them. PolicyEngine.evaluate has no awareness of lifecycle stage, so the intended metrics->autonomy->looser-policy feedback loop is not wired.

gaps_and_bugs.title: Entire H-series self-tuning layer is built but unwired
gaps_and_bugs.detail: PolicyOptimizer, ConfigAutoTuner, TemplatePromoter, PromptEvolutionEngine, and metrics trackers (GrowthHistoryRecorder, LearningCurveTracker, CoevolutionGraph, MilestoneTracker, VelocityCalculator, GroupBalanceEvaluator) are referenced ONLY by tests — no CLI command, web endpoint, scheduler, or orchestrator invokes them. The 'self-growing' value proposition is largely unreachable at runtime.

gaps_and_bugs.title: PolicyEngine auto_approve default-priority mismatch makes 'no-priority' proposals non-auto-approvable
gaps_and_bugs.detail: _check_auto_approve defaults a missing priority to 'medium' (order 1) which exceeds max_priority 'low' (order 0), returning None; but _check_human_required defaults missing priority to 'low'. Inconsistent defaults mean a proposal lacking an explicit priority can never auto-approve even if it is style/doc/comment.

gaps_and_bugs.title: LangGraph checkpoint DB is cwd-relative and connection leaks
gaps_and_bugs.detail: build_self_improvement_graph hardcodes checkpointer_path='self_improvement_checkpoints.db' (created in the process cwd, not ~/.pantheon) and stashes the sqlite3 connection on the graph via setattr with no close path, so repeated graph builds leak connections and scatter DB files. Violates the state-location rule (should live under ~/.pantheon).

gaps_and_bugs.title: decide_next_step can re-pickup the same proposal indefinitely until cycle cap
gaps_and_bugs.detail: After execute_improvement, decide_next_step routes back to pickup_proposals if current_proposal is truthy, but executed proposals are only marked done/failed in state_manager, not removed from the in-memory queue logic; progress relies solely on the cycle_count>=5 cap and re-query returning fewer items, which is fragile if status updates lag.

gaps_and_bugs.title: InternalConsultant ignores dynamic review intensity / metrics on most paths
gaps_and_bugs.detail: trigger.execute_task_with_strict_review and worker_executor.execute_with_full_review call run_strict_quality_review WITHOUT current_metrics, so the health-aware strictness escalation (the only consumer of cfg.review_strictness thresholds) is dead on the trigger/worker paths and only active where metrics are explicitly threaded.

improvement_ideas.title: Route ALL approvals (web + CLI) through PolicyEngine
improvement_ideas.rationale: Make _approve_proposal_internal call PolicyEngine.evaluate first so HUMAN_REQUIRED/REJECT rules are enforced uniformly, fulfilling the engine's stated 'single common path' contract and preventing risky proposals from being applied via the GUI.

improvement_ideas.title: Wire web-edited policy_rules into a live PolicyEngine
improvement_ideas.rationale: Persist GUI policy_rules to ~/.pantheon/policy.yaml (or have PolicyEngine accept a dict) so editing approval rules in settings actually changes routing behavior instead of being inert state.

improvement_ideas.title: Unify the two health-score formulas behind one service
improvement_ideas.rationale: Collapse balanced_growth and HealthCalculator into a single source of truth (or clearly name them org-health vs activity-health) so CLI/web/scheduler agree on what 'health' means and the lifecycle-bar UI can actually be shown.

improvement_ideas.title: Close the metrics->lifecycle->policy autonomy loop
improvement_ideas.rationale: Feed OrganizationLifecycle.get_auto_approve_bonus / get_review_intensity into PolicyEngine and InternalConsultant strictness so mature/autonomous orgs really do get lighter review and looser auto-approval — the core self-growth promise.

improvement_ideas.title: Schedule the H-series self-tuners in platform run-all / scheduler
improvement_ideas.rationale: Invoke PolicyOptimizer, ConfigAutoTuner, GrowthHistoryRecorder, MilestoneTracker, LearningCurveTracker after each improvement cycle so the platform records trends, awards milestones, and proposes its own threshold tweaks instead of leaving them test-only dead weight.

improvement_ideas.title: Expose SelfImprovementCycle (core meta-analysis) via CLI/web
improvement_ideas.rationale: Add e.g. `pantheon platform self-improve` and a web action that runs run_meta_analysis_cycle, surfaces core_version + HUMAN_REQUIRED meta-proposals, and lets the user approve them — currently the core-improvement loop has no user entry point.

improvement_ideas.title: Move LangGraph checkpoints under ~/.pantheon and manage the connection
improvement_ideas.rationale: Honor the state-location convention and avoid sqlite connection leaks by placing self_improvement_checkpoints.db in platform_home and using a context-managed SqliteSaver, making the HITL graph production-usable.

improvement_ideas.title: Add a dedicated self-improvement-loop test-triage subagent gap
improvement_ideas.rationale: Because the strict-review path depends on the claude CLI returning valid JSON (_generate_and_parse_json raises after retries), a Pantheon-agent/skill that validates/repairs JSON output would harden run_strict_quality_review against malformed model output in autonomous runs.

improvement_ideas.title: Make InternalConsultant strictness metrics-aware on every path
improvement_ideas.rationale: Thread current_metrics through trigger.execute_task_with_strict_review and WorkerTaskExecutor so health-driven strictness escalation actually fires, activating the config/default.yaml review_strictness thresholds that are otherwise unused.

components.name: BaseAgent / AgentTask / AgentResult
components.role: Abstract base contract for every in-app SpecialistAgent: async run(AgentTask)->AgentResult, plus skill-prompt injection and knowledge-loop hooks

components.name: AgentFactory
components.role: YAML-driven factory that turns agents/definitions/*.yaml into BaseAgent instances; uses the Python class in `implementation:` or falls back to a GenericSkillAgent built from skills+behavior

components.name: OrchestratorAgent
components.role: Central router: CLI and web call this; it delegates to PreTaskOrchestrator.analyze()+execute() rather than doing work itself

components.name: CodeReviewAgent
components.role: Reads up to 15 repo code files and asks the LLM for 3-5 structured improvement suggestions; heuristic fallback if LLM fails

components.name: ImprovementExecutorAgent
components.role: Applies one approved suggestion to a file via LLM; creates a GitHub PR if token+repo present, else a local pantheon/improvement-* git branch

components.name: CodebaseExplorerAgent
components.role: Token-minimizing repo investigator using CodebaseIndexer+CodebaseSnapshot; auto-saves findings to KnowledgeManager

components.name: GenericSkillAgent
components.role: Skill-parameterized fallback LLM agent: same class behaves as planner/researcher/auditor depending on SpecialistAgent.skills via AgentSkillEngine prompt injection

components.name: ChatAgent / ChatSession
components.role: Natural-language + slash-command front end for `pantheon chat` and the web chat API; maps intents to platform tools (init/add/analyze/approve/goal/status) and to the orchestrator path

components.name: ConversationAgent
components.role: Read-only Q&A agent that answers developer questions from persisted state (knowledge, pending proposals, org health) WITHOUT the BaseAgent contract; currently not wired to any entrypoint

components.name: ToolDesignAgent
components.role: Self-extension designer: converts a CapabilityGap into an ImplementationSpec (class name, file path, method signatures, integration points)

components.name: SelfCodeWriter
components.role: Self-extension coder: turns an ImplementationSpec into syntactically validated Python (AST-checked), detects required libs; templates agent/utility scaffolds when no LLM

components.name: Agent definitions (YAML SSoT)
components.role: Single source of truth for the 16 registerable agents (name/skills/tools/behavior/implementation/response_format); loaded by core/loaders/agent_loader.AgentLoader
key_functions: strategic_planner.yaml + 8 GenericSkillAgent-backed personas (corporate_researcher, knowledge_curator, org_designer, performance_analyst, prompt_optimizer, quality_guardian, security_auditor, workflow_designer)

entrypoints.name: BaseAgent.run(AgentTask) -> AgentResult
entrypoints.summary: The universal agent contract. Every concrete agent implements async run(); callers build an AgentTask(task_type, description, input) and receive an AgentResult(success, output, thinking_process, execution_log, error). safe_run() wraps it with standardized error handling.

entrypoints.name: OrchestratorAgent.create().run(task)
entrypoints.summary: Central CLI/web router. _get_orchestrator() retrieves a PreTaskOrchestrator (with OrchestrationPatternStore); run() calls analyze() then execute(); if no agent_factory, returns analysis-only. Used by main.py _get_orchestrator() and web _approve_proposal_internal().

entrypoints.name: pantheon chat
entrypoints.summary: commands/chat.py -> agents.chat_agent.run_chat(): interactive loop. Natural-language intents classified to code_review/codebase_exploration go through PreTaskOrchestrator; slash commands (/init /add /analyze /proposals /approve /goal /status /agents) call TOOL_HANDLERS directly. Works without an LLM via slash-only mode.

entrypoints.name: POST /api analyze (web _perform_analyze)
entrypoints.summary: web/server.py instantiates CodeReviewAgent directly (NOT via factory/orchestrator), runs it on the org repo, persists each suggestion as an ImprovementProposal. Distinct code path from the CLI orchestrator route.

entrypoints.name: approve proposal (web _approve_proposal_internal)
entrypoints.summary: Approving a proposal in the GUI runs OrchestratorAgent.create().run(task_type=improvement_execution), which routes to ImprovementExecutorAgent to apply the change and open a PR / local branch; records execution events.

entrypoints.name: chat session endpoints (web/server.py ~327, ~2933)
entrypoints.summary: Web chat reuses agents.chat_agent.ChatSession(has_llm=_has_llm()) to serve the React ChatPage; same intent/slash logic as the CLI.

entrypoints.name: SelfExtensionPipeline.run_for_gap
entrypoints.summary: Self-improvement chain: CapabilityGapAnalyzer -> ToolDesignAgent.design(gap) -> SelfCodeWriter.write_code(spec) -> SelfIntegrationTester. Note these two agents are driven by their direct design()/write_code() methods, not the run(AgentTask) contract.

flows_participated.role_in_flow: CodeReviewAgent generates suggestions (CLI via OrchestratorAgent; web via direct _perform_analyze) which are persisted as ImprovementProposals; on approval, OrchestratorAgent routes to ImprovementExecutorAgent which applies the change and creates a PR or local pantheon/improvement-* branch.

ChatSession (chat_agent.py) is the front end for `pantheon chat` and the web chat API: classifies intent, runs orchestrator tasks or slash-command tool handlers (init/add/analyze/proposals/approve/goal/status/agents).
Indirect: chat_agent's /goal and run_goal tool invoke core.goals.AbstractGoalPipeline; orchestration of generated work ultimately flows back through OrchestratorAgent/AgentFactory.

OrchestratorAgent is the single entrypoint CLI/web use; it delegates routing+execution to PreTaskOrchestrator which selects agents via AgentFactory and runs single/sequential/parallel/review-loop patterns.

ToolDesignAgent (gap -> ImplementationSpec) and SelfCodeWriter (spec -> validated Python) are the design+implement stages of SelfExtensionPipeline; QualityGuardian/SecurityAuditor YAML agents back proposal generation.
CodebaseExplorerAgent provides token-minimized repo investigation (Indexer+Snapshot) and caches results to KnowledgeManager so other agents avoid re-reading raw files.
chat_agent tool handlers (_tool_initialize_platform, _tool_add_organization) bootstrap the platform and register Organizations with a default Core division / Alpha team.
ConversationAgent answers questions from persisted state, but is currently orphaned (no CLI/web/core caller) so this flow is not actually reachable in production.

state_touched: ~/.pantheon/organizations/*.json (org definitions read by ConversationAgent._load_organizations and chat tools)
git working tree: ImprovementExecutorAgent creates pantheon/improvement-<slug>-<ts> branches and commits
KnowledgeManager store (insights saved/enriched by BaseAgent, CodeReviewAgent, CodebaseExplorerAgent)
OrchestrationPatternStore (execution patterns persisted via PreTaskOrchestrator behind OrchestratorAgent)

gaps_and_bugs.title: NameError: PROVIDER_LABEL_MAP is undefined in run_chat()
gaps_and_bugs.detail: agents/chat_agent.py:743 references PROVIDER_LABEL_MAP.get(...) but the symbol is never defined or imported anywhere in the repo (grep confirms a single occurrence — the usage). When `pantheon chat` starts AND Claude Code is available (session.has_llm True), the banner block raises NameError and crashes the interactive entrypoint. Only the no-LLM branch survives. Define PROVIDER_LABEL_MAP = {"claude_code": "Claude Code"} or drop the lookup.

gaps_and_bugs.title: ConversationAgent is orphaned (dead code)
gaps_and_bugs.detail: ConversationAgent/ConversationResponse are exported from agents/__init__.py and tested, but no CLI command, web route, or core module ever constructs or calls ConversationAgent.ask(). The intended developer-Q&A flow is unreachable in production. Either wire it to a `pantheon ask`/chat path or remove it. It also does not implement the BaseAgent.run(AgentTask) contract, so it can't be routed via AgentFactory/OrchestratorAgent.

gaps_and_bugs.title: Stale provider_name="anthropic" defaults are misleading post-F1
gaps_and_bugs.detail: CodeReviewAgent.__init__ and ImprovementExecutorAgent.__init__ default provider_name="anthropic" and call get_llm_provider(self._provider_name). After F1, get_llm_provider ignores provider_name and always returns ClaudeCodeProvider, so the arg is dead but implies a hosted-Anthropic path that no longer exists. AgentFactory.create() further passes provider_name="claude_code" only when the signature has that param, creating inconsistent provider naming. Remove the param or rename to reflect Claude Code.

gaps_and_bugs.title: Blocking sync LLM call inside async run()
gaps_and_bugs.detail: GenericSkillAgent.run() (async) calls llm.complete(messages) which is ClaudeCodeProvider.complete -> invoke -> run_claude_sync (a blocking subprocess call). This blocks the event loop, violating the project rule 'no blocking I/O inside async def handlers'. Should await provider.generate(...) instead. Same pattern risk in SelfCodeWriter._write_with_llm / ToolDesignAgent._design_with_llm which call self._llm.invoke (sync) — acceptable only because those are sync methods, but they are invoked from async run()/pipeline contexts.

gaps_and_bugs.title: ChatSession(has_llm=...) constructor arg is silently overwritten
gaps_and_bugs.detail: web/server.py constructs ChatSession(has_llm=_has_llm()), but ChatSession.__post_init__ calls refresh_llm_config() which recomputes has_llm from _load_llm_config()/claude_available(), discarding the passed value. The explicit web-side has_llm is dead. Either honor the injected value or stop passing it.

gaps_and_bugs.title: CodeReviewAgent double fallback + provider mismatch can mask real LLM errors
gaps_and_bugs.detail: _generate_suggestions catches JSONDecodeError and returns [] (then run() silently substitutes heuristic templates), while other exceptions are re-raised and also caught in run() -> heuristic fallback. The net effect: a misconfigured/unavailable Claude Code yields generic template suggestions with success=True, so the GUI/CLI shows plausible-but-fake proposals instead of surfacing the failure.

gaps_and_bugs.title: Two divergent analyze paths (web direct vs CLI orchestrator)
gaps_and_bugs.detail: web/server.py _perform_analyze instantiates CodeReviewAgent directly with hardcoded skills [DEEP_RESEARCH, PERFORMANCE_ANALYSIS], bypassing OrchestratorAgent/PreTaskOrchestrator, while CLI/chat route through the orchestrator. The two paths can diverge in skill injection, knowledge enrichment, and pattern learning, making behavior inconsistent between GUI and CLI.

gaps_and_bugs.title: ConversationAgent known-issue counting can double count / mislead
gaps_and_bugs.detail: _count_known_issues returns len(knowledge_hits) when any hits exist, otherwise counts warn/critical notifications — it never combines them and a single keyword can inflate hits, so the '危険/リスク' answer's issue count is not a reliable metric.

improvement_ideas.title: Unify all generation entrypoints behind OrchestratorAgent/AgentFactory
improvement_ideas.rationale: web/server.py instantiates CodeReviewAgent directly and SelfExtensionPipeline calls design()/write_code() directly, bypassing the run(AgentTask)->AgentResult contract and PreTaskOrchestrator learning. Routing everything through the factory would give consistent skill injection, knowledge enrichment, pattern learning, and error semantics across CLI/web/self-extension.

improvement_ideas.title: Add a `pantheon ask` CLI command and web route wired to ConversationAgent
improvement_ideas.rationale: ConversationAgent is implemented, exported, and tested but never invoked. A read-only Q&A entrypoint over persisted state (proposals/health/knowledge) is a low-risk, high-value feature that just needs a command + dispatch + route.

improvement_ideas.title: Remove/rename dead provider_name plumbing
improvement_ideas.rationale: provider_name="anthropic"/"claude_code" args in CodeReviewAgent, ImprovementExecutorAgent, and AgentFactory are vestigial post-F1 (get_llm_provider ignores them). Cleaning them removes the false impression of a hosted-Anthropic path and simplifies the factory's signature-inspection branch.

improvement_ideas.title: Make GenericSkillAgent fully async (await generate)
improvement_ideas.rationale: Replace the blocking llm.complete() call with `await provider.generate(...)` to honor the no-blocking-in-async rule and avoid stalling the FastAPI event loop during chat/orchestrated tasks.

improvement_ideas.title: Surface LLM-unavailable instead of silent heuristic suggestions
improvement_ideas.rationale: CodeReviewAgent falls back to canned template suggestions with success=True when Claude Code is unavailable. Add a 'fallback=true'/'degraded' flag in output (and propagate to GUI) so users know proposals are heuristic stubs, not real review output.

improvement_ideas.title: Add a generate-tests sub-agent to the self-extension chain
improvement_ideas.rationale: SelfCodeWriter emits run() stubs with TODO placeholders and SelfIntegrationTester only validates syntax. A TestWriter agent that emits a pytest skeleton per generated module would close the loop toward genuinely self-validating extensions.

improvement_ideas.title: Wire QualityGuardian YAML agent to an actual change hook
improvement_ideas.rationale: quality_guardian.yaml describes autonomous checks on *.tsx/server.py/package.json changes and scripts/check_help_coverage.py, but nothing triggers it. A Claude Code Stop/PostToolUse hook (or a `pantheon guard` command) could actually run those checks and auto-file proposals as the YAML intends.

improvement_ideas.title: Add an agents-subsystem regression test for entrypoint wiring
improvement_ideas.rationale: Bugs like the undefined PROVIDER_LABEL_MAP slipped through because run_chat()'s LLM-available banner is untested. A smoke test that imports every agent module and exercises run_chat's banner path (mocking claude_available True) would catch NameErrors and dead-symbol regressions.

components.name: organization data model
components.role: Pydantic v2 models for the whole org hierarchy + quality/proposal records (the persisted shape everything else (de)serializes)

components.name: PlatformStateManager (global ~/.pantheon)
components.role: Global store: registers/loads Organizations under ~/.pantheon/organizations, platform.json config, shared knowledge, and hands out per-repo state managers

components.name: RepoStateManager (repo <repo>/.pantheon)
components.role: Per-repository JSON state: proposals, reviews, decisions, sessions, org copies, current_state, cross-org snapshot

components.name: SQLiteStateManager
components.role: WAL-mode SQLite store for proposals/decisions/insights with safe query() — concurrency-safe alternative to JSON, but only the read path (pantheon query) is wired in

components.name: StateMigrator
components.role: One-shot JSON->SQLite migration of legacy proposals.json/decisions.json — orphaned (no CLI/web caller)

components.name: BackupManager
components.role: Timestamped copytree/copy backups of platform state with list/restore/cleanup; backs the platform backup/restore CLI

components.name: SystemDoctor
components.role: Health diagnosis of ~/.pantheon: SQLite integrity_check, missing dirs, oversized JSON; auto-fixes missing dirs (pantheon doctor)

components.name: bootstrap
components.role: First-run initialization: creates/repairs the system Meta-Improvement Organization, wires it to the Core repo, initializes platform.json, writes default policy.yaml

components.name: org_factory
components.role: Builds Organization->Division->Team->SpecialistAgent trees from config/departments/*.yaml (or a minimal fallback), normalizing skills to the 2-3 contract

components.name: meta_improvement template
components.role: YAML defining the 6 divisions/teams/required-skills that org_factory expands into the system Meta-Improvement Organization

components.name: PolicyEngine (default policy)
components.role: Approval routing engine; bootstrap calls save_default_policy() to seed ~/.pantheon/policy.yaml (auto_approve / human_required / auto_reject rules)

entrypoints.name: pantheon platform status / run-all / config / logs / backup / restore
entrypoints.summary: Cross-org dashboard, priority-ordered improvement cycles, config get/set, log tail, and platform-home backup/restore

entrypoints.name: FastAPI /api/organizations + proposals/goals/sessions routes
entrypoints.summary: PlatformStateManager-backed REST/WS server; _psm() also auto-promotes system orgs (is_system) on every access

entrypoints.name: bootstrap_platform() / ensure_meta_improvement_org()
entrypoints.summary: Idempotent platform initializer invoked by init and org add; ensures the system org + policy + platform.json exist

flows_participated.role_in_flow: bootstrap_platform() creates the Meta-Improvement Organization from meta_improvement.yaml, wires it to the Core repo, writes platform.json + default policy.yaml under ~/.pantheon (the state foundation everything else assumes)
org_factory builds the Organization/Division/Team/SpecialistAgent tree; PlatformStateManager.save_organization persists it as ~/.pantheon/organizations/<uuid>.json

RepoStateManager is the proposal store: save_improvement_proposal writes <repo>/.pantheon/improvements/<uuid>.json, get_pending_improvement_proposals lists active ones, update_proposal_status walks the proposed->in_progress->done/failed/rejected lifecycle
PlatformStateManager.load_organizations + get_org_state_manager feed SelfImprovementLoop per org; GroupHQState/metrics aggregate health for prioritization
_psm() (PlatformStateManager) backs all org/proposal REST endpoints; _migrate_system_orgs re-applies the is_system flag on load
RepoStateManager.save_session_context/load_session_context/list_session_contexts persists cross-session shared context under <repo>/.pantheon/sessions
BackupManager snapshots platform-home; SystemDoctor diagnoses and repairs ~/.pantheon integrity
_migrate_legacy_home copies ~/.repocorp -> ~/.pantheon on first run; StateMigrator (unwired) would move legacy JSON -> SQLite

state_touched: ~/.pantheon/state.db — SQLite proposals/decisions/insights (read by pantheon query; SystemDoctor integrity_check)

gaps_and_bugs.title: CLI org remove can delete the system Meta-Improvement Organization
gaps_and_bugs.detail: cmd_org_remove in commands/org.py:146-160 calls psm.remove_organization(str(org.id)) without checking org.is_system, violating the Organization.is_system 'システム組織（削除不可）' contract. The web path guards this: DELETE /api/organizations (web/server.py:2236) returns 403 for is_system orgs. The CLI has no such guard, so 'pantheon org remove --name "Meta-Improvement Organization" --yes' destroys the system org and orphans platform.json's meta_improvement_org_id.

gaps_and_bugs.title: SQLite store is write-orphaned: pantheon query reads a DB nothing populates
gaps_and_bugs.detail: No production write path ever instantiates SQLiteStateManager.save_improvement_proposal — analyze (commands/org.py cmd_analyze), apply, the web server, the daemon, and SelfImprovementLoop all write JSON via RepoStateManager. The only SQLite writer is StateMigrator, which has NO CLI/web caller. So ~/.pantheon/state.db stays empty and 'pantheon query' returns nothing unless a user manually runs an unexposed migrator. SystemDoctor also integrity_checks a DB that may never be created.

gaps_and_bugs.title: RepoStateManager 'recent' proposal ordering is by random UUID, not time
gaps_and_bugs.detail: get_pending_improvement_proposals (core/state/manager.py:124) does sorted(improvements_dir.glob('*.json'), reverse=True) where filenames are uuid4() (not time-sortable), then breaks at the limit. So with >limit proposals you get an arbitrary subset, not the most recent — created_at is ignored. Same UUID-filename pattern affects reviews. record_decision/get_recent_decisions correctly sort by timestamp, so the inconsistency is real.

gaps_and_bugs.title: analyze writes proposals to CWD/.pantheon when target_repo_path is unset
gaps_and_bugs.detail: cmd_analyze (commands/org.py:175) does repo_path = Path(org.target_repo_path) if set else Path('.'), and constructs RepoStateManager(repo_path,...). For an org with no repo, proposals are written under the current working directory's .pantheon, while proposals/list/apply via psm.get_org_state_manager fall back to platform_home (core/platform/state.py:164). The read and write locations diverge, so generated proposals can become invisible to 'pantheon proposals'.

gaps_and_bugs.title: JSON and SQLite proposal stores can silently diverge / no single source of truth
gaps_and_bugs.detail: ImprovementProposal lifecycle is duplicated across RepoStateManager (per-repo JSON, used by analyze/apply/web) and SQLiteStateManager (global ~/.pantheon/state.db, used by query). Status updates flow only through the JSON store; the SQLite copy (if ever migrated) is never updated by apply/reject, so 'pantheon query status=done' would report stale data. There is no reconciliation.

gaps_and_bugs.title: Legacy-home migration is silent and partial on failure
gaps_and_bugs.detail: _migrate_legacy_home (core/platform/state.py:21) shutil.copytree(~/.repocorp -> ~/.pantheon) swallows OSError with 'pass'. A partially-copied tree leaves ~/.pantheon existing-but-incomplete, and because the guard is 'if new_home.exists(): return', the migration will never retry — potentially losing legacy orgs/policy with no warning.

gaps_and_bugs.title: PolicyEngine default constructed without a path never loads ~/.pantheon/policy.yaml
gaps_and_bugs.detail: PolicyEngine(policy_path=None) loads DEFAULT_POLICY and ignores the seeded ~/.pantheon/policy.yaml that bootstrap writes. Any caller that does PolicyEngine() (rather than PolicyEngine(platform_home/'policy.yaml')) silently bypasses user policy edits, so the file bootstrap creates may have no effect depending on the caller.

gaps_and_bugs.title: get_org_state_manager fallback aliases all repo-less orgs to one shared platform_home store
gaps_and_bugs.detail: When target_repo_path is empty, get_org_state_manager (core/platform/state.py:164) returns RepoStateManager(platform_home, org.name). Multiple repo-less orgs then share the same ~/.pantheon/improvements + decisions namespace keyed only by filename UUIDs, so their proposals/decisions are commingled with no per-org separation.

improvement_ideas.title: Add is_system guard to CLI org remove (and a --force escape hatch)
improvement_ideas.rationale: Mirror the web 403 guard in cmd_org_remove so the system Meta-Improvement Organization can't be deleted via CLI. Centralize the check in PlatformStateManager.remove_organization (raise on is_system unless force=True) so both CLI and API share one rule.

improvement_ideas.title: Unify proposal persistence behind one StateStore interface
improvement_ideas.rationale: Two parallel proposal stores (RepoStateManager JSON vs SQLiteStateManager) with overlapping APIs invite drift; the SQLite write path is dead. Define a single ProposalStore protocol, make SQLite the canonical backend (concurrency-safe, queryable), and have analyze/apply/web/daemon write through it so 'pantheon query' reflects reality.

improvement_ideas.title: Expose StateMigrator as 'pantheon migrate' and call it from bootstrap
improvement_ideas.rationale: StateMigrator is fully implemented and tested but has no entrypoint, so legacy JSON never reaches SQLite. A 'pantheon migrate' subcommand (and an opportunistic one-shot run in bootstrap_platform) would activate the SQLite store the query command and SystemDoctor already assume exists.

improvement_ideas.title: Sort proposals/reviews by created_at, embed timestamp in filename
improvement_ideas.rationale: Fix the UUID-ordering bug by either sorting loaded proposals on the created_at field before applying the limit, or naming files <isoTimestamp>_<uuid>.json so glob+reverse yields true recency. Prevents arbitrary truncation of 'recent' results.

improvement_ideas.title: Resolve analyze's repo path through get_org_state_manager
improvement_ideas.rationale: cmd_analyze should obtain its RepoStateManager via psm.get_org_state_manager(org) (same as proposals/apply/web) instead of constructing one against Path('.'), eliminating the write-here/read-there split for repo-less orgs.

improvement_ideas.title: Add a state-integrity CC subagent / pantheon doctor --deep that cross-checks stores
improvement_ideas.rationale: Extend SystemDoctor to detect JSON<->SQLite divergence, orphaned proposals, and repo-less-org commingling, with auto-fix where safe. Surfaces the silent inconsistencies above before they corrupt run-all prioritization.

improvement_ideas.title: Schema-version the persisted models and add a forward-migration step
improvement_ideas.rationale: GroupHQState.version is '0.3.0-reorg' and PLATFORM_VERSION is '1.0.0' but nothing reconciles or migrates older Organization JSON on load (load_organizations just skips on validation error, silently dropping data). A versioned migrate-on-read keeps old ~/.pantheon stores loadable after model changes.

improvement_ideas.title: Make legacy-home migration loud and resumable
improvement_ideas.rationale: Log/print on _migrate_legacy_home success/failure and, on partial copy, move the incomplete dir aside (e.g. .pantheon.partial) so the existence guard doesn't permanently block a retry, preventing silent loss of legacy orgs and policy.

subsystem: runtime (core/runtime — Claude Code generation backend + multiplexer/session orchestration)

components.name: ClaudeCodeProvider / run_claude
components.role: The ONLY generation backend: shells out to the local `claude -p --output-format json` CLI; duck-types the old LLMProvider interface so all agents keep working with no API keys

components.name: rate_limit
components.role: Detects Claude 5h/weekly usage-limit messages in captured agent output and computes a best-effort, clamped reset time so the orchestrator can auto-resume instead of hard-failing

components.name: SessionOrchestrator
components.role: Maps a Pantheon session->multiplexer workspace and each agent->surface; launches/polls/stops/resumes headless `claude` agents and persists all state under <repo>/.pantheon/sessions/<id>/

components.name: MultiplexerDriver contract
components.role: Abstract driver interface + data models (AgentSpec, Surface, Workspace, SurfaceStatus) the orchestrator speaks to; concrete drivers implement workspace/surface lifecycle

components.name: get_driver factory
components.role: Selects wmux (Windows) / cmux (macOS) / headless backend; honors PANTHEON_MULTIPLEXER and always falls back to HeadlessDriver so a session can always run

components.name: HeadlessDriver
components.role: Always-available substrate (CI/tests/no-GUI): runs each agent as a subprocess.Popen writing to a per-agent log; cross-process liveness via OS pid check

components.name: WmuxDriver
components.role: Windows GUI driver: one wmux workspace per agent via mcp.claimWorkspace, types the `claude` command with input.send, polls exit codes via terminal.readEvents, tags panes via pane.setMetadata

components.name: WmuxClient (RPC)
components.role: Low-level newline-delimited JSON-RPC client for the wmux app-control endpoint (TCP 127.0.0.1:<~/.wmux-tcp-port> or named pipe); lazy identify+declarePermissions handshake and one-time user approval

components.name: CmuxDriver
components.role: macOS sibling: subclasses WmuxDriver, repoints the RPC client at ~/.cmux.sock + ~/.cmux-auth-token; not yet validated against live cmux

entrypoints.name: pantheon session start/list/show/stop/resume/doctor
entrypoints.summary: commands/session.py: drives multiplexer-backed agent sessions; --demo/--agents-file build AgentTasks, --watch polls and auto-resumes rate-limited agents; `doctor` reports claude CLI + wmux connectivity

entrypoints.name: GET/POST /api/sessions, /api/sessions/runtime, /api/sessions/{id}, /api/sessions/{id}/agents/{agent_id}/log, /api/sessions/{id}/stop|resume
entrypoints.summary: web/server.py session endpoints: list/create/inspect/stop/resume sessions for the AI operation dashboard and report live claude/wmux/driver status; broadcasts session_* events over the updates WebSocket

entrypoints.name: get_llm_provider() -> ClaudeCodeProvider
entrypoints.summary: core/llm facade: every agent/component that does generation resolves to ClaudeCodeProvider; provider.generate/ainvoke/invoke/complete run the local claude CLI, raising ClaudeUnavailableError offline so agents fall back to heuristics

entrypoints.name: _require_api_key() / _has_llm() gate
entrypoints.summary: main.py and web/server.py gate LLM-dependent CLI commands and chat on claude_available() (the legacy 'api key' name now just checks the CLI is installed/logged-in)

flows_participated.flow_name: Generation backbone (analyze->propose, abstract-goal pipeline, self-improvement, etc.)
flows_participated.role_in_flow: ClaudeCodeProvider is the single execution path for ALL 'thinking' across the product; via core.llm.get_llm_provider() every agent (code_review, improvement_executor, codebase_explorer, chat, tool_design, self_code_writer, generic_skill) calls .generate/.ainvoke which shells to `claude -p`. Offline it raises ClaudeUnavailableError and agents use heuristic fallbacks.

SessionOrchestrator + multiplexer drivers ARE this flow: start_session creates a workspace and launches each AgentTask as a headless `claude` surface, persists session.json + per-agent prompt/system/log files, polls exit codes, and auto-resumes rate-limited agents. Surfaced via `pantheon session` CLI and /api/sessions.
web /api/chat and the /ws chat WebSocket route through ChatSession(has_llm=_has_llm()); _has_llm()/claude_available() decides whether responses come from the claude CLI or a heuristic stub.

core/ui/setup_wizard.py and `pantheon session doctor` use claude_available()/claude_binary() and is_wmux_running()/WmuxClient.verify() to tell the user whether the claude CLI is installed and whether the wmux plugin is approved.

Indirect: commands/platform.py run-all and the daemon run org improvement cycles through agents that use ClaudeCodeProvider; they do NOT currently use SessionOrchestrator/multiplexer surfaces (a missed integration — see improvement ideas).

state_touched: wmux pane metadata custom keys pantheon.agentId / pantheon.session (set via pane.setMetadata)
tests_covering: tests/conftest.py (sets PANTHEON_NO_CLAUDE=1 session-wide so claude_available() is False)

gaps_and_bugs.title: Headless cross-process poll fabricates DONE without exit code, hiding failures + defeating rate-limit auto-resume
gaps_and_bugs.detail: HeadlessDriver.poll_surface (core/runtime/multiplexer/headless_driver.py ~line 138-143): when there is no in-memory Popen handle (the common case for the web server / `session show`, which construct a fresh orchestrator+driver per request), a finished pid is unconditionally marked SurfaceStatus.DONE with exit_code left None. A `claude` agent that exited non-zero or hit a usage limit is therefore reported as successfully DONE. Because SessionOrchestrator.poll_session only runs detect_rate_limit() on surfaces whose status == FAILED, a rate-limited headless agent is never flagged RATE_LIMITED and never auto-resumes when polled from a different process than the one that launched it.

gaps_and_bugs.title: Headless agents are orphaned across processes; web-launched sessions cannot be stopped
gaps_and_bugs.detail: HeadlessDriver keeps Popen handles only in-memory (self._procs). The web server launches via asyncio.to_thread(orch.start_session,...) and then handles /api/sessions/{id}/stop on a brand-new orchestrator/driver instance whose _procs is empty, so close_surface is a no-op and the underlying `claude` process keeps running. _reattach_driver for headless also cannot terminate by pid (only checks liveness). Long-running headless agents can leak.

gaps_and_bugs.title: detect_rate_limit only runs on FAILED surfaces, but rate-limit exit may surface as DONE/exit 0
gaps_and_bugs.detail: SessionOrchestrator.poll_session (line ~228) calls detect_rate_limit only when surface.status == FAILED. The `claude` CLI may print a usage-limit message and still exit 0 (or the headless cross-process path forces DONE). Those agents are recorded completed instead of rate_limited, so resume_session/due_for_resume never act on them. Rate-limit detection should also scan logs of DONE/exit-0 surfaces.

gaps_and_bugs.title: ClaudeCodeProvider.generate silently ignores temperature, max_tokens, tools, tool_choice
gaps_and_bugs.detail: core/runtime/claude_code.py: generate() accepts temperature/max_tokens/tools/tool_choice for interface compatibility but passes none of them to run_claude (only model/cwd/timeout). Callers that rely on low temperature, token caps, or tool definitions get default `claude` behavior with no warning — a correctness/expectation gap for any agent that thinks it constrained the model.

gaps_and_bugs.title: wmux readEvents 'lastCommandOnly' can report the launching command's exit, not the agent's
gaps_and_bugs.detail: WmuxDriver.open_surface types the `claude` command into a freshly claimed workspace's shell via input.send. poll_surface reads terminal.readEvents lastCompletedRange. If the shell prompt/init command completes before claude finishes, lastCompletedRange.exitCode may reflect an earlier command, prematurely marking the surface DONE/FAILED. There is no correlation between the typed command and the completed range.

gaps_and_bugs.title: ClaudeCodeProvider.stream() does not actually stream; provider-path system prompt not threaded
gaps_and_bugs.detail: ClaudeCodeProvider.stream() just awaits generate() and yields the full content once — there is no real token streaming despite the codebase using --output-format stream-json elsewhere. generate() also never forwards a separate system kwarg or extra_args to run_claude, so a system prompt passed outside the messages list isn't honored on the provider path.

gaps_and_bugs.title: _pwsh_command uses 2>&1 | Tee-Object which produces UTF-16LE logs the parser must guess
gaps_and_bugs.detail: SessionOrchestrator._pwsh_command pipes through PowerShell Tee-Object (UTF-16LE, sometimes without BOM). _read_log then heuristically sniffs NUL density to decode — fragile for short logs or logs with binary-ish content, risking garbled rate-limit detection from wmux-driven agents.

gaps_and_bugs.title: WmuxClient pipe transport opens one connection per call with byte-at-a-time pipe reads
gaps_and_bugs.detail: wmux_rpc._read_line_pipe reads one byte at a time and _send_once opens/closes the pipe per RPC. Under the poll loop (every 2s per agent in --watch) this is slow and can race the Electron endpoint; there is no persistent connection or pipelining.

improvement_ideas.title: Persist headless pids + a reattachable process registry so stop/poll work cross-process
improvement_ideas.rationale: Store each headless agent's pid (already in pty_id) plus a way to terminate it (os.kill / taskkill) and have HeadlessDriver.close_surface honor pty_id when _procs is empty. Fixes the orphaned-process and 'cannot stop web-launched session' bugs and makes poll_surface report real completion.

improvement_ideas.title: Read the headless agent's real exit code from the JSON/stream-json log on cross-process poll
improvement_ideas.rationale: Instead of fabricating DONE, parse the trailing stream-json result line (which carries result + is_error/subtype) to recover exit status and feed detect_rate_limit, so rate-limited agents auto-resume regardless of which process polls them.

improvement_ideas.title: Run rate-limit detection on every terminal surface (DONE and FAILED) and on exit-0
improvement_ideas.rationale: Move detect_rate_limit out of the FAILED-only branch in poll_session so usage-limit messages that accompany a clean exit still mark the agent RATE_LIMITED with a retry_at — the whole auto-resume feature depends on catching these.

improvement_ideas.title: Wire SessionOrchestrator into platform run-all / daemon
improvement_ideas.rationale: run-all and the autonomous daemon currently call agents directly via ClaudeCodeProvider and don't use the session/multiplexer machinery, so they don't get the dashboard, per-agent logs, or automatic rate-limit resume. Routing org improvement cycles through start_session would make the daemon visible in the AI operation dashboard and resilient to the 5h/weekly limits.

improvement_ideas.title: Honor temperature/max_tokens (and a real --append-system-prompt) in ClaudeCodeProvider.generate
improvement_ideas.rationale: Map supported kwargs to claude CLI flags (or at least log a warning when an unsupported generation param is passed) so agents that constrain the model aren't silently ignored; add a streaming path that consumes --output-format stream-json for true incremental output.

improvement_ideas.title: Background auto-resume worker that polls + resumes rate-limited sessions without --watch
improvement_ideas.rationale: Today --watch only resumes while the CLI is attached. A lightweight resume worker (or a hook on the existing daemon) that scans .pantheon/sessions for status==rate_limited and calls resume_session when due_for_resume>0 would make long multi-hour sessions truly hands-off.

improvement_ideas.title: Persistent wmux RPC connection + batched polling
improvement_ideas.rationale: Keep one open transport per WmuxClient and chunked pipe reads (instead of per-call connect + byte-at-a-time) to cut overhead and races during the 2s poll loop, and batch workspace.list once per poll cycle instead of per surface.

improvement_ideas.title: Correlate wmux command exit codes to the launched agent command
improvement_ideas.rationale: Tag the typed command (e.g. via a sentinel echo or readEvents command id) so poll_surface only treats the completedRange belonging to the agent's `claude` invocation as its exit code, eliminating premature DONE/FAILED from shell-init commands.

improvement_ideas.title: Add focused driver tests for HeadlessDriver/WmuxDriver lifecycle and rate-limit resume
improvement_ideas.rationale: Current tests cover the orchestrator (with a FakeDriver), rate_limit parsing, factory selection, and wmux RPC, but there is no direct test of HeadlessDriver cross-process poll, close_surface termination, or the poll_session->detect_rate_limit->resume_session path — exactly where the high-severity bugs live.

components.name: OrganizationDesigner
components.role: Designs Organization structure (Division/Team/SpecialistAgent) from a purpose string; heuristic keyword fallback + optional llm_client; YAML template save/load; team optimization.

components.name: OrgWizard
components.role: Conversational 3-step org-setup wizard (Japanese prompts) that turns answers into purpose/scale/focus + a suggested org name.

components.name: CapabilityGapDetector
components.role: Counts repeated issue categories and proposes new Divisions once a category crosses a threshold (default 5).

components.name: CrossOrgCollaborator
components.role: In-memory request/accept registry for cross-Organization collaboration (no persistence).

components.name: DivisionCoordinator
components.role: Picks 2 divisions for a cross-division task via keyword heuristics and tracks the resulting CrossDivisionTask in memory.

components.name: OrgSelfDiagnostics
components.role: Computes strengths/weaknesses/next-steps from health score + accepted/rejected/knowledge counts and formats a CLI diagnostic report.

components.name: OrgGoalManager
components.role: Persists per-org goals to ~/.pantheon/org_goals.json and derives category weight boosts (2.0 for goal categories) to bias proposal prioritization.

components.name: OrgSnapshotManager
components.role: Serializes Organization to JSON snapshots under ~/.pantheon/org_snapshots/ and restores org_data for time-travel/rollback of org structure.

components.name: DeveloperProfileManager
components.role: Learns the developer's approval/rejection patterns per category, communication style, preferred/avoided/weak categories; emits personalization context to bias proposal generation.

components.name: ActivityTracker
components.role: Appends CLI command run timestamps to activity_log.jsonl and computes active-hours / peak-day work patterns.

components.name: DeveloperGrowthTracker
components.role: Appends test-ratio/approval-rate growth snapshots to developer_growth.jsonl and summarizes the trend.

components.name: DeveloperGoalManager
components.role: Persists personal developer goals (metric/target) and maps goal text to priority categories.

components.name: GrowthReporter
components.role: Builds monthly growth reports (score delta, accepted/knowledge counts) and motivational messages.

components.name: MultiUserManager
components.role: Resolves the active user via PANTHEON_USER env var and the per-user profile path; lists users.

components.name: KnowledgeManager
components.role: Persists cross-org insights/best-practices to <repo>/.pantheon/knowledge/; tag/importance/usage tracking, archival, and prompt-ready context for agents.

components.name: KnowledgeClassifier
components.role: Keyword-based auto-tagging of knowledge entries into security/performance/maintainability/testing/architecture domains.

components.name: FailurePatternRegistry
components.role: Records recurring failed-change patterns (category x file-glob) to ~/.pantheon/failure_patterns.json and suppresses proposals after >=3 occurrences.

components.name: EventDetector
components.role: Scans all Organizations for NEW_COMMIT (git rev-parse), HEALTH_DROP, and PENDING_SPIKE events; caches last-seen commit SHAs; drives the autonomous scheduler.

components.name: SecurityAuditor
components.role: Regex-based lightweight scanner for api-key exposure, eval/exec, os.system, pickle.loads across Python files.

components.name: ProactiveNotifier
components.role: Generates warn/critical notifications on health drops and proposal backlog; persists to notifications.jsonl.

components.name: SafeChangeExecutor
components.role: Backs up a file, writes the new content, runs pytest, and rolls back on failure; enforces path-stays-within-project-root.

components.name: DiffQualityReviewer
components.role: Flags risky diffs: removed docstrings, added TODO/FIXME, file shrinkage, public-signature changes; gates acceptance on no error-severity issues.

components.name: ImpactAnalyzer
components.role: Builds an import dependency graph and BFS-finds dependents (depth 3) to rate change impact low/medium/high and require human review.

components.name: ChangeSizeController
components.role: Estimates changed line count, decides if a change exceeds MAX_LINES_PER_CHANGE (100) and hints how to split a proposal.

components.name: ASTAnalyzer
components.role: Parses a Python file into functions/classes/imports and locates a function's line for targeted edits.

components.name: LintChecker
components.role: Runs ruff (E,W) on changed files, falling back to py_compile if ruff is absent.

components.name: MultiFileChangeCoordinator
components.role: Validates multi-file edits for broken imports and changed public signatures to keep cross-file changes consistent.

components.name: AgentLoader
components.role: Loads agent definitions from agents/definitions/*.yaml into AgentDefinition objects (skills/tools/behavior/response_format) and builds system prompts; lazy singleton.

components.name: SkillLoader
components.role: Loads skill definitions from skills/*.yaml into SkillDefinition objects and renders prompt addons (persona/focus/output_hint); lazy singleton.

components.name: schema_support
components.role: Validates and normalizes schema_version on loaded YAML (only '1.0' supported), warning otherwise.

components.name: DocGenerator
components.role: Extracts module/class/function docstrings via AST and renders Markdown docs for a file or whole directory.

components.name: HealthReportGenerator
components.role: Builds weekly health reports (issues + recommendations) from metrics and formats them as CLI or HTML.

components.name: RichDashboard
components.role: Renders org summary/proposals table/org tree as a rich TUI with graceful plain-text fallback when rich is unavailable.

components.name: SetupWizard
components.role: Onboarding checklist that checks claude CLI backend availability, org existence, repo registration, and first-analysis completion.

components.name: InteractiveApprover
components.role: CLI helper to list pending proposals, show detail, and parse a/d/s keystrokes into approve/reject/skip actions.

components.name: ErrorMessageHelper
components.role: Maps error codes/exceptions to friendly Japanese messages with help text (e.g. MISSING_API_KEY -> claude CLI guidance).

components.name: I18n
components.role: Tiny PANTHEON_LANG-driven ja/en translation table with ja fallback.

entrypoints.name: GET /api/agents and GET /api/skills
entrypoints.summary: FastAPI endpoints in web/server.py serialize YAML-defined agents/skills by calling AgentLoader().all() and SkillLoader().all() (the only web-exposed entrypoints in this subsystem).

entrypoints.name: AutonomousScheduler loop
entrypoints.summary: core/scheduler.py drives the self-improvement daemon by calling EventDetector.detect_all() each interval (NEW_COMMIT/HEALTH_DROP/PENDING_SPIKE) then routing through PolicyEngine.

entrypoints.name: OrganizationDesigner via OrgInstantiator
entrypoints.summary: core/goals/org_instantiator.py instantiates OrganizationDesigner to design/reuse an Organization for a structured goal in the abstract-goal pipeline.

entrypoints.name: KnowledgeManager agent enrichment
entrypoints.summary: agents/base.py BaseAgent injects KnowledgeManager.get_context_for_agent() pre-run and persists learnings via save_insight() (B-01), and core/platform/state.py wires the shared knowledge store.

entrypoints.name: SafeChangeExecutor via self-extension pipeline
entrypoints.summary: core/intelligence/self_extension_pipeline.py uses SafeChangeExecutor to apply backed-up, test-verified, auto-rollback code changes when Pantheon extends itself.

entrypoints.name: Loaders via CapabilityRegistry / AgentFactory
entrypoints.summary: core/intelligence/capability_registry.py and agents/agent_factory.py load AgentLoader/SkillLoader to register YAML-defined capabilities and build GenericSkillAgents without code changes.

flows_participated.role_in_flow: OrganizationDesigner (via OrgInstantiator) designs or reuses an Organization structure from the parsed goal; OrgGoalManager category weights can bias which work is prioritized.

KnowledgeManager enriches analysis prompts and stores learnings; DeveloperProfileManager personalizes proposals from approval history; execution/* (SafeChangeExecutor, DiffQualityReviewer, ImpactAnalyzer, ChangeSizeController, MultiFileChangeCoordinator, LintChecker, ASTAnalyzer) gate/apply the change; FailurePatternRegistry suppresses repeatedly-failing proposals; InteractiveApprover renders the approval UI.
EventDetector is the trigger source for the AutonomousScheduler; ProactiveNotifier surfaces health/backlog alerts; SafeChangeExecutor applies auto-approved self-extension changes.

OrgWizard and OrganizationDesigner build/templatize org structure; OrgSnapshotManager versions it; CapabilityGapDetector and DivisionCoordinator/CrossOrgCollaborator model org evolution and cross-org work.
SetupWizard provides the step checklist (claude backend, org, repo, first analysis); ErrorMessageHelper and I18n shape user-facing guidance.
AgentLoader/SkillLoader back GET /api/agents and GET /api/skills; HealthReportGenerator/RichDashboard provide reporting surfaces (dashboard rendering is library-side, not currently routed).
ActivityTracker, DeveloperGrowthTracker, DeveloperGoalManager, GrowthReporter, MultiUserManager track and report per-developer growth (data layer only; no wired entrypoint).

gaps_and_bugs.title: Most of misccore is dead/unreachable from any user entrypoint
gaps_and_bugs.detail: All of core/profile/*, core/monitoring/*, core/security/*, and all of core/ui/* (doc_generator, health_report_generator, rich_dashboard, setup_wizard, interactive_approver, error_messages, i18n), plus most of core/hierarchy/* (org_wizard, org_diagnostics, org_goals, org_snapshot, capability_gap_detector, cross_org_collaborator, division_coordinator) and most of core/execution/* (diff_reviewer, impact_analyzer, change_size_controller, ast_analyzer, lint_checker, multi_file_coordinator) are imported ONLY by tests. Production import grep over main.py/commands/web/agents/core finds only loaders, knowledge.manager, hierarchy.org_designer, execution.safe_executor, and events.detector. The bulk of this subsystem is per-feature-ticket code (D-/E-/F-/I-/J- IDs) with no CLI/web wiring, so users cannot reach the dashboard, setup wizard, interactive approver, notifications, security audit, growth reports, or org diagnostics.

gaps_and_bugs.title: MultiUserManager profile path diverges from DeveloperProfileManager
gaps_and_bugs.detail: MultiUserManager.get_profile_path returns <platform_home>/profiles/<user>/developer_profile.json, but DeveloperProfileManager reads/writes <platform_home>/developer_profiles/<user>.json. The two never agree, so multi-user switching (PANTHEON_USER) does not actually scope the learned profile that the rest of the system uses; per-user personalization is effectively broken.

gaps_and_bugs.title: ActivityTracker uses timezone-naive datetime.now()
gaps_and_bugs.detail: record_activity calls datetime.now() (no tz) violating the repo-wide 'always datetime.now(timezone.utc)' rule enforced by hooks/AGENTS.md. Hour/day-of-week buckets are therefore in server-local time and not comparable across environments; also inconsistent with every other persisted timestamp in the subsystem.

gaps_and_bugs.title: OrgSelfDiagnostics.format_report assumes emoji-capable encoding
gaps_and_bugs.detail: format_report and several reporters/notifiers emit raw emoji (Japanese CLI strings). On the documented Windows dev environment with cp932/legacy console encodings this can raise UnicodeEncodeError on print, and the module has no entrypoint/test that exercises actual stdout, so the failure mode is latent.

gaps_and_bugs.title: SafeChangeExecutor runs the FULL pytest suite for every single change
gaps_and_bugs.detail: _run_tests invokes 'pytest tests/ -q' over the entire suite with a 120s timeout per applied change. For the self-extension pipeline this is O(suite) per file and, given the documented 6 pre-existing Windows failures, returncode!=0 / failed>0 will make EVERY change look failed-and-rolled-back on Windows even when the change is fine. There is no way to scope tests to the impacted files.

gaps_and_bugs.title: EventDetector recomputes for detect_for_org and mutates commit cache as a side effect
gaps_and_bugs.detail: detect_for_org calls detect_all() (scanning every org and running git rev-parse on each) then filters by name, and detect_all() persists _last_commits as a side effect. A per-org query thus rescans all orgs and can consume/advance NEW_COMMIT events for unrelated orgs, making single-org detection both expensive and stateful in surprising ways.

gaps_and_bugs.title: FailurePatternRegistry.get_patterns sort is confusing/likely wrong
gaps_and_bugs.detail: get_patterns sorts with key=lambda p:(-p.occurrence_count, p.last_seen) AND reverse=False — the negative occurrence_count already ascends-by-most-frequent, but combining a negated numeric key with a string secondary and reverse=False yields a non-obvious ordering (most-frequent first, then oldest last_seen first). Likely not the intended 'most frequent, most recent' ordering.

gaps_and_bugs.title: SecurityAuditor api_key_exposure regex risks false positives and misses quoted secrets
gaps_and_bugs.detail: The api_key_exposure rule matches bare prefixes (sk-/ghp_/AIza) anywhere in a line, so documentation/comments and example placeholders flag as 'high', while it cannot catch generic high-entropy secrets. As the only security gate in the subsystem with no allowlist, it is noisy and easily bypassed.

improvement_ideas.title: Wire the existing UI/diagnostics modules to real CLI subcommands
improvement_ideas.rationale: RichDashboard, SetupWizard, InteractiveApprover, HealthReportGenerator, OrgSelfDiagnostics and ProactiveNotifier are fully built and tested but unreachable. Add `pantheon dashboard`, `pantheon setup`, `pantheon approve` (interactive), `pantheon health-report`, `pantheon diagnose` subcommands (via the /add-cli-command pattern) to turn ~15 dead modules into shipped features at low cost.

improvement_ideas.title: Unify developer-profile storage paths
improvement_ideas.rationale: Make DeveloperProfileManager honor MultiUserManager.get_profile_path (or vice-versa) so PANTHEON_USER actually scopes the learned profile. Fixes a real correctness bug and unlocks the advertised multi-user personalization (D-10).

improvement_ideas.title: Scope SafeChangeExecutor tests to impacted files via ImpactAnalyzer
improvement_ideas.rationale: SafeChangeExecutor and ImpactAnalyzer live in the same package but don't cooperate. Use the import graph to run only dependent test modules (or a targeted node-id set) instead of the whole suite, making the self-extension verify loop fast and immune to the 6 known unrelated Windows failures.

improvement_ideas.title: Add a feature that composes the execution/* gates into one ChangeGate
improvement_ideas.rationale: DiffQualityReviewer, ImpactAnalyzer, ChangeSizeController, MultiFileChangeCoordinator and LintChecker are independent helpers. A single ChangeGate orchestrator (lint -> size -> diff quality -> impact -> multi-file consistency -> safe-execute) would give the improvement-executor a coherent pre-apply pipeline and a single decision object.

improvement_ideas.title: Expose EventDetector/ProactiveNotifier state over the web API
improvement_ideas.rationale: The daemon detects events and writes notifications.jsonl, but the web GUI has no /api/notifications or /api/events endpoint. Surfacing recent notifications and detected events would make the autonomous self-improvement loop observable in the UI (the documented HUMAN_REQUIRED review path).

improvement_ideas.title: Replace timezone-naive datetime in ActivityTracker and add a lint hook
improvement_ideas.rationale: ActivityTracker.record_activity violates the repo's tz-aware rule. Fix it and extend the existing PreToolUse guard / ruff config to flag bare datetime.now() so this class of regression is caught automatically rather than relying on prose.

improvement_ideas.title: Make detect_for_org a cheap, side-effect-free query
improvement_ideas.rationale: Add a per-org detection path that scans only the requested org and does not persist the global commit cache, so single-org event checks (e.g. from the web API or a targeted CLI) are fast and don't swallow other orgs' NEW_COMMIT events.

improvement_ideas.title: Promote the YAML loaders to a documented extension mechanism + schema CLI
improvement_ideas.rationale: AgentLoader/SkillLoader already enable code-free agent/skill definitions with schema_version validation, but only '1.0' is supported and there's no validation command. A `pantheon validate-defs` command (and a CC skill update) would let contributors add agents/skills via YAML safely and catch schema drift before runtime warnings.
